{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_d1bc873197f84e72acd193a1","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Rules extraction: distill session patterns into agent-specific rules files.\n2\t//!\n3\t//! Uses frequency-based heuristics (no LLM) to extract:\n4\t//! - Tool/command preferences (e.g. pnpm over npm)\n5\t//! - Language/framework conventions\n6\t//! - Test directory patterns\n7\t//! - Common error pitfalls\n8\t//! - User corrections (\"don't use X, use Y\")\n9\t\n10\tuse std::collections::HashMap;\n11\tuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\n12\t\n13\tuse regex::Regex;\n14\t\n15\tuse crate::error::Result;\n16\tuse crate::event::Role;\n17\tuse crate::scraper::Scraper;\n18\t\n19\tuse std::sync::LazyLock;\n20\t\n21\t/// Regex patterns for detecting user corrections in conversation content.\n22\tstatic CORRECTION_PATTERNS: LazyLock<Vec<Regex>> = LazyLock::new(|| {\n23\t    vec![\n24\t        // \"don't use X, use Y\" / \"don't use X. Use Y\"\n25\t        Regex::new(r\"(?i)don'?t use\\s+(\\S+).*?(?:use|try|prefer)\\s+(\\S+)\").unwrap(),\n26\t        // \"use X instead of Y\" / \"use X instead. not Y\"\n27\t        Regex::new(r\"(?i)(?:use|try|prefer)\\s+(\\S+)\\s+instead\\s+of\\s+(\\S+)\").unwrap(),\n28\t        // \"always use X\" / \"never use Y\"\n29\t        Regex::new(r\"(?i)always\\s+(?:use|prefer)\\s+(\\S+)\").unwrap(),\n30\t        Regex::new(r\"(?i)never\\s+(?:use|prefer)\\s+(\\S+)\").unwrap(),\n31\t        // \"prefer X over Y\"\n32\t        Regex::new(r\"(?i)prefer\\s+(\\S+)\\s+over\\s+(\\S+)\").unwrap(),\n33\t        // \"X is preferred\" / \"Y is not preferred\"\n34\t        Regex::new(r\"(?i)(\\S+)\\s+is\\s+(?:not\\s+)?preferred\").unwrap(),\n35\t    ]\n36\t});\n37\t\n38\t/// Map of competing tool pairs (if both appear, the more frequent one wins).\n39\tstatic COMPETING_TOOLS: LazyLock<Vec<(&'static str, &'static str)>> = LazyLock::new(|| {\n40\t    vec![\n41\t        (\"pnpm\", \"npm\"),\n42\t        (\"pnpm\", \"yarn\"),\n43\t        (\"yarn\", \"npm\"),\n44\t        (\"bun\", \"npm\"),\n45\t        (\"cargo\", \"rustup\"),\n46\t        (\"pip\", \"conda\"),\n47\t        (\"pytest\", \"unittest\"),\n48\t        (\"vitest\", \"jest\"),\n49\t        (\"pnpm\", \"bun\"),\n50\t    ]\n51\t});\n52\t\n53\t/// A single extracted rule.\n54\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n55\tpub enum Rule {\n56\t    /// \"Use X\" convention\n57\t    Convention(String),\n58\t    /// \"Avoid X\" warning\n59\t    Warning(String),\n60\t    /// Project context (language, framework, build tool)\n61\t    Context(String),\n62\t    /// User correction (\"don't use X, use Y\")\n63\t    Correction(String),\n64\t}\n65\t\n66\timpl Rule {\n67\t    fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n68\t        match self {\n69\t            Rule::Convention(s) => s,\n70\t            Rule::Warning(s) => s,\n71\t            Rule::Context(s) => s,\n72\t            Rule::Correction(s) => s,\n73\t        }\n74\t    }\n75\t}\n76\t\n77\t/// Aggregated rule extraction results.\n78\tpub struct RulesOutput {\n79\t    pub rules: Vec<Rule>,\n80\t    pub project_path: PathBuf,\n81\t    pub sessions_analyzed: usize,\n82\t}\n83\t\n84\t/// Output format for rules files.\n85\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\n86\tpub enum OutputFormat {\n87\t    Claude,\n88\t    Cursor,\n89\t    Aider,\n90\t}\n91\t\n92\timpl OutputFormat {\n93\t    #[allow(clippy::should_implement_trait)]\n94\t    pub fn from_str(s: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n95\t        match s.to_lowercase().as_str() {\n96\t            \"claude\" => Some(OutputFormat::Claude),\n97\t            \"cursor\" => Some(OutputFormat::Cursor),\n98\t            \"aider\" => Some(OutputFormat::Aider),\n99\t            _ => None,\n100\t        }\n101\t    }\n102\t\n103\t    /// Default output filename for this format.\n104\t    pub fn filename(&self) -> &'static str {\n105\t        match self {\n106\t            OutputFormat::Claude => \"CLAUDE.md\",\n107\t            OutputFormat::Cursor => \".cursorrules\",\n108\t            OutputFormat::Aider => \".aider.conf.yml\",\n109\t        }\n110\t    }\n111\t}\n112\t\n113\t/// Extract rules from all sessions matching a project path.\n114\tpub fn extract_rules(data_dir: &Path, project_path: &Path) -> Result<RulesOutput> {\n115\t    let mut scraper = Scraper::new(data_dir.to_path_buf())?;\n116\t    scraper.load_plugins()?;\n117\t\n118\t    let project_str = project_path.to_string_lossy().to_string();\n119\t    let project_str_normalized = if project_str.ends_with('/') {\n120\t        project_str.clone()\n121\t    } else {\n122\t        format!(\"{}/\", project_str)\n123\t    };\n124\t\n125\t    let all_sessions = scraper.all_sessions()?;\n126\t    let mut rules: Vec<Rule> = Vec::new();\n127\t    let mut sessions_analyzed = 0;\n128\t\n129\t    // Accumulators for frequency analysis\n130\t    let mut bash_cmd_counts: HashMap<String, usize> = HashMap::new();\n131\t    let mut code_fence_langs: HashMap<String, usize> = HashMap::new();\n132\t    let mut file_extensions: HashMap<String, usize> = HashMap::new();\n133\t    let mut tool_counts: HashMap<String, usize> = HashMap::new();\n134\t    let mut error_fingerprints: HashMap<String, usize> = HashMap::new();\n135\t    let mut test_dirs: HashMap<String, usize> = HashMap::new();\n136\t    let mut corrections: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n137\t\n138\t    for session_id in &all_sessions {\n139\t        let events = match scraper.read_session(session_id) {\n140\t            Ok(e) => e,\n141\t            Err(_) => continue,\n142\t        };\n143\t\n144\t        if events.is_empty() {\n145\t            continue;\n146\t        }\n147\t\n148\t        // Filter sessions by project path\n149\t        let session_project = events.iter().find_map(|e| e.project.clone());\n150\t        let matches_project = match &session_project {\n151\t            Some(p) => p.starts_with(&project_str_normalized) || p == &project_str,\n152\t            None => false,\n153\t        };\n154\t\n155\t        if !matches_project {\n156\t            continue;\n157\t        }\n158\t\n159\t        sessions_analyzed += 1;\n160\t\n161\t        for event in &events {\n162\t            // Bash commands\n163\t            if event.role == Role::ToolCall && event.tool.as_deref() == Some(\"Bash\") {\n164\t                let mut tokens = event.content.split_whitespace();\n165\t                let cmd = tokens.next().unwrap_or(\"\");\n166\t                if cmd == \"$\" || cmd == \"#\" {\n167\t                    if let Some(actual) = tokens.next() {\n168\t                        *bash_cmd_counts.entry(actual.to_string()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n169\t                    }\n170\t                } else {\n171\t                    *bash_cmd_counts.entry(cmd.to_string()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n172\t                }\n173\t            }\n174\t\n175\t            // Tool names\n176\t            if event.role == Role::ToolCall {\n177\t                if let Some(ref tool) = event.tool {\n178\t                    *tool_counts.entry(tool.to_lowercase()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n179\t                }\n180\t            }\n181\t\n182\t            // Code fence languages\n183\t            for cap in CODE_FENCE_RE.captures_iter(&event.content) {\n184\t                if let Some(lang) = cap.get(1) {\n185\t                    let lang = lang.as_str().to_lowercase();\n186\t                    if !lang.is_empty() && lang != \"text\" && lang != \"plaintext\" {\n187\t                        *code_fence_langs.entry(lang).or_insert(0) += 1;\n188\t                    }\n189\t                }\n190\t            }\n191\t\n192\t            // File extensions\n193\t            for fp in &event.file_paths {\n194\t                if let Some(ext) = fp.rsplit('.').next() {\n195\t                    *file_extensions.entry(ext.to_lowercase()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n196\t                }\n197\t                // Detect test directories\n198\t                for component in fp.split('/') {\n199\t                    if component == \"tests\"\n200\t                        || component == \"__tests__\"\n201\t                        || component == \"spec\"\n202\t                        || component == \"specs\"\n203\t                    {\n204\t                        *test_dirs.entry(component.to_string()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n205\t                    }\n206\t                    if component.starts_with(\"test_\") || component.ends_with(\"_test\") {\n207\t                        *test_dirs.entry(format!(\"{}_files\", component)).or_insert(0) += 1;\n208\t                    }\n209\t                }\n210\t            }\n211\t\n212\t            // Error fingerprints\n213\t            for fp in &event.error_fingerprints {\n214\t                *error_fingerprints.entry(fp.clone()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n215\t            }\n216\t\n217\t            // User corrections\n218\t            if event.role == Role::User {\n219\t                extract_corrections(&event.content, &mut corrections);\n220\t            }\n221\t        }\n222\t    }\n223\t\n224\t    // --- Generate rules from frequency analysis ---\n225\t\n226\t    // 1. Language/framework context (top 3 code fence languages)\n227\t    let mut langs: Vec<_> = code_fence_langs.iter().collect();\n228\t    langs.sort_by(|a, b| b.1.cmp(a.1));\n229\t    for (lang, count) in langs.iter().take(3) {\n230\t        if **count >= 2 {\n231\t            rules.push(Rule::Context(format!(\"Primary language: {}\", lang)));\n232\t        }\n233\t    }\n234\t\n235\t    // 2. Tool/command preferences (competing tools)\n236\t    for (preferred, disfavored) in COMPETING_TOOLS.iter() {\n237\t        let pref_count = bash_cmd_counts.get(*preferred).copied().unwrap_or(0);\n238\t        let disf_count = bash_cmd_counts.get(*disfavored).copied().unwrap_or(0);\n239\t\n240\t        if pref_count > 0 && pref_count > disf_count {\n241\t            rules.push(Rule::Convention(format!(\n242\t                \"Use {} instead of {} (seen {}x vs {}x)\",\n243\t                preferred, disfavored, pref_count, disf_count\n244\t            )));\n245\t        } else if pref_count > 0 && disf_count == 0 {\n246\t            rules.push(Rule::Convention(format!(\n247\t                \"Use {} ({} sessions, {} never used)\",\n248\t                preferred, pref_count, disfavored\n249\t            )));\n250\t        }\n251\t    }\n252\t\n253\t    // 3. Build system detection\n254\t    let build_tools = [\n255\t        \"cargo\", \"make\", \"cmake\", \"npm\", \"pnpm\", \"yarn\", \"bun\", \"go\", \"gcc\", \"clang\",\n256\t    ];\n257\t    for tool in &build_tools {\n258\t        if let Some(count) = bash_cmd_counts.get(*tool) {\n259\t            if *count >= 2 {\n260\t                rules.push(Rule::Context(format!(\"Build system: {}\", tool)));\n261\t            }\n262\t        }\n263\t    }\n264\t\n265\t    // 4. Test patterns\n266\t    if !test_dirs.is_empty() {\n267\t        let top_test: Vec<_> = test_dirs\n268\t            .iter()\n269\t            .max_by_key(|(_, c)| *c)\n270\t            .map(|(k, _)| k.clone())\n271\t            .into_iter()\n272\t            .collect();\n273\t        if let Some(dir) = top_test.first() {\n274\t            if dir.ends_with(\"_files\") {\n275\t                rules.push(Rule::Context(format!(\n276\t                    \"Test files follow {} naming convention\",\n277\t                    dir.replace(\"_files\", \"\")\n278\t                )));\n279\t            } else {\n280\t                rules.push(Rule::Context(format!(\"Tests in {} directory\", dir)));\n281\t            }\n282\t        }\n283\t    }\n284\t\n285\t    // 5. Common error pitfalls (top 5 recurring errors)\n286\t    let mut top_errors: Vec<_> = error_fingerprints.iter().collect();\n287\t    top_errors.sort_by(|a, b| b.1.cmp(a.1));\n288\t    for (fp, count) in top_errors.iter().take(5) {\n289\t        if **count >= 2 {\n290\t            let short_fp = truncate_fp(fp, 100);\n291\t            rules.push(Rule::Warning(format!(\n292\t                \"Watch for recurring error: {} ({} occurrences)\",\n293\t                short_fp, count\n294\t            )));\n295\t        }\n296\t    }\n297\t\n298\t    // 6. User corrections\n299\t    for correction in &corrections {\n300\t        rules.push(Rule::Correction(correction.clone()));\n301\t    }\n302\t\n303\t    // Deduplicate rules\n304\t    let mut seen = std::collections::HashSet::new();\n305\t    rules.retain(|r| seen.insert(r.as_str().to_string()));\n306\t\n307\t    // Sort: corrections first, then conventions, then warnings, then context\n308\t    rules.sort_by(|a, b| match (a, b) {\n309\t        (Rule::Correction(_), _) => std::cmp::Ordering::Less,\n310\t        (_, Rule::Correction(_)) => std::cmp::Ordering::Greater,\n311\t        (Rule::Convention(_), _) => std::cmp::Ordering::Less,\n312\t        (_, Rule::Convention(_)) => std::cmp::Ordering::Greater,\n313\t        (Rule::Warning(_), _) => std::cmp::Ordering::Less,\n314\t        (_, Rule::Warning(_)) => std::cmp::Ordering::Greater,\n315\t        _ => std::cmp::Ordering::Equal,\n316\t    });\n317\t\n318\t    Ok(RulesOutput {\n319\t        rules,\n320\t        project_path: project_path.to_path_buf(),\n321\t        sessions_analyzed,\n322\t    })\n323\t}\n324\t\n325\tstatic CODE_FENCE_RE: LazyLock<Regex> =\n326\t    LazyLock::new(|| Regex::new(r\"```([a-zA-Z0-9_+#-]+)\").unwrap());\n327\t\n328\t/// Extract correction patterns from user messages.\n329\tfn extract_corrections(content: &str, corrections: &mut Vec<String>) {\n330\t    for pattern in CORRECTION_PATTERNS.iter() {\n331\t        if let Some(caps) = pattern.captures(content) {\n332\t            // Build a readable correction string\n333\t            let full_match = caps.get(0).unwrap().as_str().trim();\n334\t            // Clean up: remove trailing punctuation, limit length\n335\t            let cleaned = full_match.trim_end_matches(['.', '!', '?', ',']).trim();\n336\t\n337\t            if cleaned.len() >= 5\n338\t                && cleaned.len() <= 200\n339\t                && !corrections.contains(&cleaned.to_string())\n340\t            {\n341\t                corrections.push(cleaned.to_string());\n342\t            }\n343\t        }\n344\t    }\n345\t}\n346\t\n347\t/// Truncate a fingerprint for display.\n348\tfn truncate_fp(fp: &str, max_len: usize) -> String {\n349\t    if fp.len() <= max_len {\n350\t        return fp.to_string();\n351\t    }\n352\t    if let Some(colon_pos) = fp.find(':') {\n353\t        let prefix = &fp[..=colon_pos];\n354\t        let remaining = max_len.saturating_sub(prefix.len() + 4);\n355\t        if remaining > 10 {\n356\t            return format!(\n357\t                \"{}{}...\",\n358\t                prefix,\n359\t                &fp[colon_pos + 1..colon_pos + 1 + remaining]\n360\t            );\n361\t        }\n362\t    }\n363\t    format!(\"{}...\", &fp[..max_len.saturating_sub(3)])\n364\t}\n365\t\n366\t/// Format rules as CLAUDE.md content (additive sections).\n367\tpub fn format_claude(output: &RulesOutput) -> String {\n368\t    let mut sections = Vec::new();\n369\t\n370\t    let corrections: Vec<_> = output\n371\t        .rules\n372\t        .iter()\n373\t        .filter_map(|r| match r {\n374\t            Rule::Correction(s) => Some(s.as_str()),\n375\t            _ => None,\n376\t        })\n377\t        .collect();\n378\t\n379\t    let conventions: Vec<_> = output\n380\t        .rules\n381\t        .iter()\n382\t        .filter_map(|r| match r {\n383\t            Rule::Convention(s) => Some(s.as_str()),\n384\t            _ => None,\n385\t        })\n386\t        .collect();\n387\t\n388\t    let warnings: Vec<_> = output\n389\t        .rules\n390\t        .iter()\n391\t        .filter_map(|r| match r {\n392\t            Rule::Warning(s) => Some(s.as_str()),\n393\t            _ => None,\n394\t        })\n395\t        .collect();\n396\t\n397\t    let context: Vec<_> = output\n398\t        .rules\n399\t        .iter()\n400\t        .filter_map(|r| match r {\n401\t            Rule::Context(s) => Some(s.as_str()),\n402\t            _ => None,\n403\t        })\n404\t        .collect();\n405\t\n406\t    if !corrections.is_empty() {\n407\t        sections.push(format!(\n408\t            \"## User Corrections\\n\\n{}\",\n409\t            corrections\n410\t                .iter()\n411\t                .map(|c| format!(\"- {}\", c))\n412\t                .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n413\t                .join(\"\\n\")\n414\t        ));\n415\t    }\n416\t\n417\t    if !conventions.is_empty() {\n418\t        sections.push(format!(\n419\t            \"## Conventions\\n\\n{}\",\n420\t            conventions\n421\t                .iter()\n422\t                .map(|c| format!(\"- {}\", c))\n423\t                .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n424\t                .join(\"\\n\")\n425\t        ));\n426\t    }\n427\t\n428\t    if !context.is_empty() {\n429\t        sections.push(format!(\n430\t            \"## Project Context\\n\\n{}\",\n431\t            context\n432\t                .iter()\n433\t                .map(|c| format!(\"- {}\", c))\n434\t                .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n435\t                .join(\"\\n\")\n436\t        ));\n437\t    }\n438\t\n439\t    if !warnings.is_empty() {\n440\t        sections.push(format!(\n441\t            \"## Known Issues\\n\\n{}\",\n442\t            warnings\n443\t                .iter()\n444\t                .map(|w| format!(\"- {}\", w))\n445\t                .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n446\t                .join(\"\\n\")\n447\t        ));\n448\t    }\n449\t\n450\t    if sections.is_empty() {\n451\t        return String::new();\n452\t    }\n453\t\n454\t    let header = format!(\n455\t        \"<!-- Auto-generated by AgentScribe rules ({} sessions analyzed) -->\\n\",\n456\t        output.sessions_analyzed\n457\t    );\n458\t\n459\t    format!(\"{}\\n{}\\n\", header, sections.join(\"\\n\\n\"))\n460\t}\n461\t\n462\t/// Format rules as .cursorrules content (one instruction per line).\n463\tpub fn format_cursor(output: &RulesOutput) -> String {\n464\t    if output.rules.is_empty() {\n465\t        return String::new();\n466\t    }\n467\t\n468\t    let lines: Vec<String> = output\n469\t        .rules\n470\t        .iter()\n471\t        .map(|r| match r {\n472\t            Rule::Convention(s) => s.clone(),\n473\t            Rule::Correction(s) => s.clone(),\n474\t            Rule::Warning(s) => format!(\"Watch out: {}\", s),\n475\t            Rule::Context(s) => s.clone(),\n476\t        })\n477\t        .collect();\n478\t\n479\t    format!(\n480\t        \"# Auto-generated by AgentScribe rules ({} sessions analyzed)\\n{}\\n\",\n481\t        output.sessions_analyzed,\n482\t        lines.join(\"\\n\")\n483\t    )\n484\t}\n485\t\n486\t/// Format rules as .aider.conf.yml content.\n487\tpub fn format_aider(output: &RulesOutput) -> String {\n488\t    if output.rules.is_empty() {\n489\t        return String::new();\n490\t    }\n491\t\n492\t    let mut conventions: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n493\t    let mut context: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n494\t\n495\t    for rule in &output.rules {\n496\t        match rule {\n497\t            Rule::Convention(s) | Rule::Correction(s) | Rule::Warning(s) => {\n498\t                conventions.push(s.clone());\n499\t            }\n500\t            Rule::Context(s) => {\n501\t                context.push(s.clone());\n502\t            }\n503\t        }\n504\t    }\n505\t\n506\t    let mut yaml = String::new();\n507\t\n508\t    if !context.is_empty() {\n509\t        // Build read-only files list from context\n510\t        yaml.push_str(\"read: []\\n\");\n511\t    }\n512\t\n513\t    if !conventions.is_empty() {\n514\t        let msg = conventions.join(\"\\\\n\");\n515\t        yaml.push_str(&format!(\"message: |\\n  {}\\n\", msg.replace('\\n', \"\\n  \")));\n516\t    }\n517\t\n518\t    format!(\n519\t        \"# Auto-generated by AgentScribe rules ({} sessions analyzed)\\n{}\\n\",\n520\t        output.sessions_analyzed, yaml\n521\t    )\n522\t}\n523\t\n524\t/// Write rules to a file in the specified format, additive (appends if file exists).\n525\tpub fn write_rules(\n526\t    output: &RulesOutput,\n527\t    format: OutputFormat,\n528\t    project_path: &Path,\n529\t) -> Result<PathBuf> {\n530\t    let content = match format {\n531\t        OutputFormat::Claude => {\n537\t        return Err(crate::error::AgentScribeError::Rules(\n538\t            \"No rules extracted from session data\".to_string(),\n539\t        ));\n540\t    }\n541\t\n542\t    let output_path = project_path.join(format.filename());\n543\t\n544\t    let final_content = if output_path.exists() {\n545\t        let existing = std::fs::read_to_string(&output_path).unwrap_or_default();\n546\t        replace_or_prepend(&existing, &content, format)\n547\t    } else {\n548\t        content\n549\t    };\n550\t\n551\t    // Ensure parent directory exists\n552\t    if let Some(parent) = output_path.parent() {\n553\t        std::fs::create_dir_all(parent)?;\n554\t    }\n555\t\n556\t    std::fs::write(&output_path, &final_content)?;\n557\t\n558\t    Ok(output_path)\n559\t}\n560\t\n561\t/// Replace an existing generated block, or prepend if not found.\n562\tfn replace_or_prepend(existing: &str, new_content: &str, format: OutputFormat) -> String {\n563\t    let marker = match format {\n564\t        OutputFormat::Claude => \"<!-- Auto-generated by AgentScribe rules\",\n565\t        OutputFormat::Cursor | OutputFormat::Aider => \"# Auto-generated by AgentScribe rules\",\n566\t    };\n567\t\n568\t    if !existing.contains(marker) {\n569\t        // No existing generated block — prepend new content\n570\t        return format!(\"{}\\n{}\", new_content.trim_end(), existing);\n571\t    }\n572\t\n573\t    // Find the start of the existing generated block\n574\t    let start = match existing.find(marker) {\n575\t        Some(s) => s,\n576\t        None => return format!(\"{}\\n{}\", new_content.trim_end(), existing),\n577\t    };\n578\t\n579\t    // Find the end: first non-generated line after the marker.\n580\t    // Generated lines are those that belong to our output format's sections.\n581\t    let generated_sections: Vec<&str> = match format {\n582\t        OutputFormat::Claude => vec![\n583\t            \"User Corrections\",\n584\t            \"Conventions\",\n585\t            \"Project Context\",\n586\t            \"Known Issues\",\n587\t        ],\n588\t        OutputFormat::Cursor | OutputFormat::Aider => vec![],\n589\t    };\n590\t\n591\t    let remaining = &existing[start..];\n592\t    let mut end = remaining.len();\n593\t\n594\t    for (i, line) in remaining.split('\\n').enumerate() {\n595\t        if i == 0 {\n596\t            continue; // skip the marker line itself\n597\t        }\n598\t        let trimmed = line.trim();\n599\t\n600\t        // Empty lines within generated block are ok\n601\t        if trimmed.is_empty() {\n602\t            continue;\n603\t        }\n604\t\n605\t        // For CLAUDE.md: check if this ## heading is NOT one of ours\n606\t        if format == OutputFormat::Claude && trimmed.starts_with(\"## \") {\n607\t            let section_name = trimmed.trim_start_matches(\"## \").trim();\n608\t            if !generated_sections.contains(&section_name) {\n609\t                end = start + remaining[..].find(line).unwrap();\n610\t                break;\n611\t            }\n612\t            continue;\n613\t        }\n614\t\n615\t        // For cursor/aider: any non-empty, non-comment line that isn't part of our\n616\t        // generated format marks the end\n617\t        if format != OutputFormat::Claude {\n618\t            // Skip known generated prefixes\n619\t            if trimmed.starts_with('#') || trimmed.starts_with(\"Watch out:\") {\n620\t                continue;\n621\t            }\n622\t            end = start + remaining[..].find(line).unwrap();\n623\t            break;\n624\t        }\n625\t    }\n626\t\n627\t    if end >= remaining.len() {\n628\t        // Generated block extends to end of file\n629\t        new_content.trim_end().to_string()\n630\t    } else {\n631\t        format!(\"{}\\n{}\", new_content.trim_end(), &existing[end..])\n632\t    }\n633\t}\n634\t\n635\t/// Format rules output for human-readable terminal display.\n636\tpub fn format_human(output: &RulesOutput) -> String {\n637\t    if output.rules.is_empty() {\n638\t        return format!(\n639\t            \"No rules extracted from {} session(s) for {}\\n\",\n640\t            output.sessions_analyzed,\n641\t            output.project_path.display()\n642\t output.sessions_analyzed,\n650\t        output.project_path.display()\n651\t    ));\n652\t\n653\t    let mut last_type = String::new();\n654\t\n655\t    for rule in &output.rules {\n656\t        let Rule::Context(s) => (\"Context\", s.as_str()),\n660\t            Rule::Warning(s) => (\"Warning\", s.as_str()),\n661\t        };\n662\t\n663\t        if label != last_type {\n664\t            if !last_type.is_empty() {\n665\t                lines.push(String::new());\n666\t            }\n667\t            lines.push(format!(\"  [{}]\", label));\n668\t            last_type = label.to_string();\n669\t        }\n670\t\n671\t        lines.push(format!(\"    - {}\", text));\n672\t    }\n673\t\n674\t    lines.join(\"\\n\")\n675\t}\n676\t\n677\t/// Extract rules and return them project_path)?;\n680\t\n681\t    if output.rules.is_empty() {\n682\t        return Ok(String::new());\n683\t    }\n684\t\n685\t    // Use CLAUDE.md format as the inline representation\n686\t    let Rules extraction: distill session patterns into agent-specific rules files.\n//!\n//! Uses frequency-based heuristics (no LLM) to extract:\n//! - Tool/command preferences (e.g. pnpm over npm)\n//! - Language/framework conventions\n//! - Test directory patterns\n//! - Common error pitfalls\n//! - User corrections (\"don't use X, use Y\")\n\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\n\nuse regex::Regex;\n\nuse crate::error::Result;\nuse crate::event::Role;\nuse crate::scraper::Scraper;\n\nuse std::sync::LazyLock;\n\n/// Regex patterns for detecting user corrections in conversation content.\nstatic CORRECTION_PATTERNS: LazyLock<Vec<Regex>> = LazyLock::new(|| {\n    vec![\n        // \"don't use X, use Y\" / \"don't use X. Use Y\"\n        Regex::new(r\"(?i)don'?t use\\s+(\\S+).*?(?:use|try|prefer)\\s+(\\S+)\").unwrap(),\n        // \"use X instead of Y\" / \"use X instead. not Y\"\n        Regex::new(r\"(?i)(?:use|try|prefer)\\s+(\\S+)\\s+instead\\s+of\\s+(\\S+)\").unwrap(),\n        // \"always use X\" / \"never use Y\"\n        Regex::new(r\"(?i)always\\s+(?:use|prefer)\\s+(\\S+)\").unwrap(),\n        Regex::new(r\"(?i)never\\s+(?:use|prefer)\\s+(\\S+)\").unwrap(),\n        // \"prefer X over Y\"\n        Regex::new(r\"(?i)prefer\\s+(\\S+)\\s+over\\s+(\\S+)\").unwrap(),\n        // \"X is preferred\" / \"Y is not preferred\"\n        Regex::new(r\"(?i)(\\S+)\\s+is\\s+(?:not\\s+)?preferred\").unwrap(),\n    ]\n});\n\n/// Map of competing tool pairs (if both appear, the more frequent one wins).\nstatic COMPETING_TOOLS: LazyLock<Vec<(&'static str, &'static str)>> = LazyLock::new(|| {\n    vec![\n        (\"pnpm\", \"npm\"),\n        (\"pnpm\", \"yarn\"),\n        (\"yarn\", \"npm\"),\n        (\"bun\", \"npm\"),\n        (\"cargo\", \"rustup\"),\n        (\"pip\", \"conda\"),\n        (\"pytest\", \"unittest\"),\n        (\"vitest\", \"jest\"),\n        (\"pnpm\", \"bun\"),\n    ]\n});\n\n/// A single extracted rule.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub enum Rule {\n    /// \"Use X\" convention\n    Convention(String),\n    /// \"Avoid X\" warning\n    Warning(String),\n    /// Project context (language, framework, build tool)\n    Context(String),\n    /// User correction (\"don't use X, use Y\")\n    Correction(String),\n}\n\nimpl Rule {\n    fn as_str(&self) -> &str {\n        match self {\n            Rule::Convention(s) => s,\n            Rule::Warning(s) => s,\n            Rule::Context(s) => s,\n            Rule::Correction(s) => s,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Aggregated rule extraction results.\npub struct RulesOutput {\n    pub rules: Vec<Rule>,\n    pub project_path: PathBuf,\n    pub sessions_analyzed: usize,\n}\n\n/// Output format for rules files.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]\npub enum OutputFormat {\n    Claude,\n    Cursor,\n    Aider,\n}\n\nimpl OutputFormat {\n    #[allow(clippy::should_implement_trait)]\n    pub fn from_str(s: &str) -> Option<Self> {\n        match s.to_lowercase().as_str() {\n            \"claude\" => Some(OutputFormat::Claude),\n            \"cursor\" => Some(OutputFormat::Cursor),\n            \"aider\" => Some(OutputFormat::Aider),\n            _ => None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Default output filename for this format.\n    pub fn filename(&self) -> &'static str {\n        match self {\n            OutputFormat::Claude => \"CLAUDE.md\",\n            OutputFormat::Cursor => \".cursorrules\",\n            OutputFormat::Aider => \".aider.conf.yml\",\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Extract rules from all sessions matching a project path.\npub fn extract_rules(data_dir: &Path, project_path: &Path) -> Result<RulesOutput> {\n    let mut scraper = Scraper::new(data_dir.to_path_buf())?;\n    scraper.load_plugins()?;\n\n    let project_str = project_path.to_string_lossy().to_string();\n    let project_str_normalized = if project_str.ends_with('/') {\n        project_str.clone()\n    } else {\n        format!(\"{}/\", project_str)\n    };\n\n    let all_sessions = scraper.all_sessions()?;\n    let mut rules: Vec<Rule> = Vec::new();\n    let mut sessions_analyzed = 0;\n\n    // Accumulators for frequency analysis\n    let mut bash_cmd_counts: HashMap<String, usize> = HashMap::new();\n    let mut code_fence_langs: HashMap<String, usize> = HashMap::new();\n    let mut file_extensions: HashMap<String, usize> = HashMap::new();\n    let mut tool_counts: HashMap<String, usize> = HashMap::new();\n    let mut error_fingerprints: HashMap<String, usize> = HashMap::new();\n    let mut test_dirs: HashMap<String, usize> = HashMap::new();\n    let mut corrections: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n\n    for session_id in &all_sessions {\n        let events = match scraper.read_session(session_id) {\n            Ok(e) => e,\n            Err(_) => continue,\n        };\n\n        if events.is_empty() {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        // Filter sessions by project path\n        let session_project = events.iter().find_map(|e| e.project.clone());\n        let matches_project = match &session_project {\n            Some(p) => p.starts_with(&project_str_normalized) || p == &project_str,\n            None => false,\n        };\n\n        if !matches_project {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        sessions_analyzed += 1;\n\n        for event in &events {\n            // Bash commands\n            if event.role == Role::ToolCall && event.tool.as_deref() == Some(\"Bash\") {\n                let mut tokens = event.content.split_whitespace();\n                let cmd = tokens.next().unwrap_or(\"\");\n                if cmd == \"$\" || cmd == \"#\" {\n                    if let Some(actual) = tokens.next() {\n                        *bash_cmd_counts.entry(actual.to_string()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n                    }\n                } else {\n                    *bash_cmd_counts.entry(cmd.to_string()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Tool names\n            if event.role == Role::ToolCall {\n                if let Some(ref tool) = event.tool {\n                    *tool_counts.entry(tool.to_lowercase()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Code fence languages\n            for cap in CODE_FENCE_RE.captures_iter(&event.content) {\n                if let Some(lang) = cap.get(1) {\n                    let lang = lang.as_str().to_lowercase();\n                    if !lang.is_empty() && lang != \"text\" && lang != \"plaintext\" {\n                        *code_fence_langs.entry(lang).or_insert(0) += 1;\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            // File extensions\n            for fp in &event.file_paths {\n                if let Some(ext) = fp.rsplit('.').next() {\n                    *file_extensions.entry(ext.to_lowercase()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n                }\n                // Detect test directories\n                for component in fp.split('/') {\n                    if component == \"tests\"\n                        || component == \"__tests__\"\n                        || component == \"spec\"\n                        || component == \"specs\"\n                    {\n                        *test_dirs.entry(component.to_string()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n                    }\n                    if component.starts_with(\"test_\") || component.ends_with(\"_test\") {\n                        *test_dirs.entry(format!(\"{}_files\", component)).or_insert(0) += 1;\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Error fingerprints\n            for fp in &event.error_fingerprints {\n                *error_fingerprints.entry(fp.clone()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n            }\n\n            // User corrections\n            if event.role == Role::User {\n                extract_corrections(&event.content, &mut corrections);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // --- Generate rules from frequency analysis ---\n\n    // 1. Language/framework context (top 3 code fence languages)\n    let mut langs: Vec<_> = code_fence_langs.iter().collect();\n    langs.sort_by(|a, b| b.1.cmp(a.1));\n    for (lang, count) in langs.iter().take(3) {\n        if **count >= 2 {\n            rules.push(Rule::Context(format!(\"Primary language: {}\", lang)));\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 2. Tool/command preferences (competing tools)\n    for (preferred, disfavored) in COMPETING_TOOLS.iter() {\n        let pref_count = bash_cmd_counts.get(*preferred).copied().unwrap_or(0);\n        let disf_count = bash_cmd_counts.get(*disfavored).copied().unwrap_or(0);\n\n        if pref_count > 0 && pref_count > disf_count {\n            rules.push(Rule::Convention(format!(\n                \"Use {} instead of {} (seen {}x vs {}x)\",\n                preferred, disfavored, pref_count, disf_count\n            )));\n        } else if pref_count > 0 && disf_count == 0 {\n            rules.push(Rule::Convention(format!(\n                \"Use {} ({} sessions, {} never used)\",\n                preferred, pref_count, disfavored\n            )));\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 3. Build system detection\n    let build_tools = [\n        \"cargo\", \"make\", \"cmake\", \"npm\", \"pnpm\", \"yarn\", \"bun\", \"go\", \"gcc\", \"clang\",\n    ];\n    for tool in &build_tools {\n        if let Some(count) = bash_cmd_counts.get(*tool) {\n            if *count >= 2 {\n                rules.push(Rule::Context(format!(\"Build system: {}\", tool)));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 4. Test patterns\n    if !test_dirs.is_empty() {\n        let top_test: Vec<_> = test_dirs\n            .iter()\n            .max_by_key(|(_, c)| *c)\n            .map(|(k, _)| k.clone())\n            .into_iter()\n            .collect();\n        if let Some(dir) = top_test.first() {\n            if dir.ends_with(\"_files\") {\n                rules.push(Rule::Context(format!(\n                    \"Test files follow {} naming convention\",\n                    dir.replace(\"_files\", \"\")\n                )));\n            } else {\n                rules.push(Rule::Context(format!(\"Tests in {} directory\", dir)));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 5. Common error pitfalls (top 5 recurring errors)\n    let mut top_errors: Vec<_> = error_fingerprints.iter().collect();\n    top_errors.sort_by(|a, b| b.1.cmp(a.1));\n    for (fp, count) in top_errors.iter().take(5) {\n        if **count >= 2 {\n            let short_fp = truncate_fp(fp, 100);\n            rules.push(Rule::Warning(format!(\n                \"Watch for recurring error: {} ({} occurrences)\",\n                short_fp, count\n            )));\n        }\n    }\n\n    // 6. User corrections\n    for correction in &corrections {\n        rules.push(Rule::Correction(correction.clone()));\n    }\n\n    // Deduplicate rules\n    let mut seen = std::collections::HashSet::new();\n    rules.retain(|r| seen.insert(r.as_str().to_string()));\n\n    // Sort: corrections first, then conventions, then warnings, then context\n    rules.sort_by(|a, b| match (a, b) {\n        (Rule::Correction(_), _) => std::cmp::Ordering::Less,\n        (_, Rule::Correction(_)) => std::cmp::Ordering::Greater,\n        (Rule::Convention(_), _) => std::cmp::Ordering::Less,\n        (_, Rule::Convention(_)) => std::cmp::Ordering::Greater,\n        (Rule::Warning(_), _) => std::cmp::Ordering::Less,\n        (_, Rule::Warning(_)) => std::cmp::Ordering::Greater,\n        _ => std::cmp::Ordering::Equal,\n    });\n\n    Ok(RulesOutput {\n        rules,\n        project_path: project_path.to_path_buf(),\n        sessions_analyzed,\n    })\n}\n\nstatic CODE_FENCE_RE: LazyLock<Regex> =\n    LazyLock::new(|| Regex::new(r\"```([a-zA-Z0-9_+#-]+)\").unwrap());\n\n/// Extract correction patterns from user messages.\nfn extract_corrections(content: &str, corrections: &mut Vec<String>) {\n    for pattern in CORRECTION_PATTERNS.iter() {\n        if let Some(caps) = pattern.captures(content) {\n            // Build a readable correction string\n            let full_match = caps.get(0).unwrap().as_str().trim();\n            // Clean up: remove trailing punctuation, limit length\n            let cleaned = full_match.trim_end_matches(['.', '!', '?', ',']).trim();\n\n            if cleaned.len() >= 5\n                && cleaned.len() <= 200\n                && !corrections.contains(&cleaned.to_string())\n            {\n                corrections.push(cleaned.to_string());\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Truncate a fingerprint for display.\nfn truncate_fp(fp: &str, max_len: usize) -> String {\n    if fp.len() <= max_len {\n        return fp.to_string();\n    }\n    if let Some(colon_pos) = fp.find(':') {\n        let prefix = &fp[..=colon_pos];\n        let remaining = max_len.saturating_sub(prefix.len() + 4);\n        if remaining > 10 {\n            return format!(\n                \"{}{}...\",\n                prefix,\n                &fp[colon_pos + 1..colon_pos + 1 + remaining]\n            );\n        }\n    }\n    format!(\"{}...\", &fp[..max_len.saturating_sub(3)])\n}\n\n/// Format rules as CLAUDE.md content (additive sections).\npub fn format_claude(output: &RulesOutput) -> String {\n    let mut sections = Vec::new();\n\n    let corrections: Vec<_> = output\n        .rules\n        .iter()\n        .filter_map(|r| match r {\n            Rule::Correction(s) => Some(s.as_str()),\n            _ => None,\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    let conventions: Vec<_> = output\n        .rules\n        .iter()\n        .filter_map(|r| match r {\n            Rule::Convention(s) => Some(s.as_str()),\n            _ => None,\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    let warnings: Vec<_> = output\n        .rules\n        .iter()\n        .filter_map(|r| match r {\n            Rule::Warning(s) => Some(s.as_str()),\n            _ => None,\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    let context: Vec<_> = output\n        .rules\n        .iter()\n        .filter_map(|r| match r {\n            Rule::Context(s) => Some(s.as_str()),\n            _ => None,\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    if !corrections.is_empty() {\n        sections.push(format!(\n            \"## User Corrections\\n\\n{}\",\n            corrections\n                .iter()\n                .map(|c| format!(\"- {}\", c))\n                .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n                .join(\"\\n\")\n        ));\n    }\n\n    if !conventions.is_empty() {\n        sections.push(format!(\n            \"## Conventions\\n\\n{}\",\n            conventions\n                .iter()\n                .map(|c| format!(\"- {}\", c))\n                .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n                .join(\"\\n\")\n        ));\n    }\n\n    if !context.is_empty() {\n        sections.push(format!(\n            \"## Project Context\\n\\n{}\",\n            context\n                .iter()\n                .map(|c| format!(\"- {}\", c))\n                .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n                .join(\"\\n\")\n        ));\n    }\n\n    if !warnings.is_empty() {\n        sections.push(format!(\n            \"## Known Issues\\n\\n{}\",\n            warnings\n                .iter()\n                .map(|w| format!(\"- {}\", w))\n                .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n                .join(\"\\n\")\n        ));\n    }\n\n    if sections.is_empty() {\n        return String::new();\n    }\n\n    let header = format!(\n        \"<!-- Auto-generated by AgentScribe rules ({} sessions analyzed) -->\\n\",\n        output.sessions_analyzed\n    );\n\n    format!(\"{}\\n{}\\n\", header, sections.join(\"\\n\\n\"))\n}\n\n/// Format rules as .cursorrules content (one instruction per line).\npub fn format_cursor(output: &RulesOutput) -> String {\n    if output.rules.is_empty() {\n        return String::new();\n    }\n\n    let lines: Vec<String> = output\n        .rules\n        .iter()\n        .map(|r| match r {\n            Rule::Convention(s) => s.clone(),\n            Rule::Correction(s) => s.clone(),\n            Rule::Warning(s) => format!(\"Watch out: {}\", s),\n            Rule::Context(s) => s.clone(),\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    format!(\n        \"# Auto-generated by AgentScribe rules ({} sessions analyzed)\\n{}\\n\",\n        output.sessions_analyzed,\n        lines.join(\"\\n\")\n    )\n}\n\n/// Format rules as .aider.conf.yml content.\npub fn format_aider(output: &RulesOutput) -> String {\n    if output.rules.is_empty() {\n        return String::new();\n    }\n\n    let mut conventions: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n    let mut context: Vec<String> = Vec::new();\n\n    for rule in &output.rules {\n        match rule {\n            Rule::Convention(s) | Rule::Correction(s) | Rule::Warning(s) => {\n                conventions.push(s.clone());\n            }\n            Rule::Context(s) => {\n                context.push(s.clone());\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    let mut yaml = String::new();\n\n    if !context.is_empty() {\n        // Build read-only files list from context\n        yaml.push_str(\"read: []\\n\");\n    }\n\n    if !conventions.is_empty() {\n        let msg = conventions.join(\"\\\\n\");\n        yaml.push_str(&format!(\"message: |\\n  {}\\n\", msg.replace('\\n', \"\\n  \")));\n    }\n\n    format!(\n        \"# Auto-generated by AgentScribe rules ({} sessions analyzed)\\n{}\\n\",\n        output.sessions_analyzed, yaml\n    )\n}\n\n/// Write rules to a file in the specified format, additive (appends if file exists).\npub fn write_rules(\n    output: &RulesOutput,\n    format: OutputFormat,\n    project_path: &Path,\n) -> Result<PathBuf> {\n    let content = match format {\n        OutputFormat::Claude => format_claude(output),\n        OutputFormat::Cursor => format_cursor(output),\n        OutputFormat::Aider => format_aider(output),\n    };\n\n    if content.is_empty() {\n        return Err(crate::error::AgentScribeError::Rules(\n            \"No rules extracted from session data\".to_string(),\n        ));\n    }\n\n    let output_path = project_path.join(format.filename());\n\n    let final_content = if output_path.exists() {\n        let existing = std::fs::read_to_string(&output_path).unwrap_or_default();\n        replace_or_prepend(&existing, &content, format)\n    } else {\n        content\n    };\n\n    // Ensure parent directory exists\n    if let Some(parent) = output_path.parent() {\n        std::fs::create_dir_all(parent)?;\n    }\n\n    std::fs::write(&output_path, &final_content)?;\n\n    Ok(output_path)\n}\n\n/// Replace an existing generated block, or prepend if not found.\nfn replace_or_prepend(existing: &str, new_content: &str, format: OutputFormat) -> String {\n    let marker = match format {\n        OutputFormat::Claude => \"<!-- Auto-generated by AgentScribe rules\",\n        OutputFormat::Cursor | OutputFormat::Aider => \"# Auto-generated by AgentScribe rules\",\n    };\n\n    if !existing.contains(marker) {\n        // No existing generated block — prepend new content\n        return format!(\"{}\\n{}\", new_content.trim_end(), existing);\n    }\n\n    // Find the start of the existing generated block\n    let start = match existing.find(marker) {\n        Some(s) => s,\n        None => return format!(\"{}\\n{}\", new_content.trim_end(), existing),\n    };\n\n    // Find the end: first non-generated line after the marker.\n    // Generated lines are those that belong to our output format's sections.\n    let generated_sections: Vec<&str> = match format {\n        OutputFormat::Claude => vec![\n            \"User Corrections\",\n            \"Conventions\",\n            \"Project Context\",\n            \"Known Issues\",\n        ],\n        OutputFormat::Cursor | OutputFormat::Aider => vec![],\n    };\n\n    let remaining = &existing[start..];\n    let mut end = remaining.len();\n\n    for (i, line) in remaining.split('\\n').enumerate() {\n        if i == 0 {\n            continue; // skip the marker line itself\n        }\n        let trimmed = line.trim();\n\n        // Empty lines within generated block are ok\n        if trimmed.is_empty() {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        // For CLAUDE.md: check if this ## heading is NOT one of ours\n        if format == OutputFormat::Claude && trimmed.starts_with(\"## \") {\n            let section_name = trimmed.trim_start_matches(\"## \").trim();\n            if !generated_sections.contains(&section_name) {\n                end = start + remaining[..].find(line).unwrap();\n                break;\n            }\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        // For cursor/aider: any non-empty, non-comment line that isn't part of our\n        // generated format marks the end\n        if format != OutputFormat::Claude {\n            // Skip known generated prefixes\n            if trimmed.starts_with('#') || trimmed.starts_with(\"Watch out:\") {\n                continue;\n            }\n            end = start + remaining[..].find(line).unwrap();\n            break;\n        }\n    }\n\n    if end >= remaining.len() {\n        // Generated block extends to end of file\n        new_content.trim_end().to_string()\n    } else {\n        format!(\"{}\\n{}\", new_content.trim_end(), &existing[end..])\n    }\n}\n\n/// Format rules output for human-readable terminal display.\npub fn format_human(output: &RulesOutput) -> String {\n    if output.rules.is_empty() {\n        return format!(\n            \"No rules extracted from {} session(s) for {}\\n\",\n            output.sessions_analyzed,\n            output.project_path.display()\n        );\n    }\n\n    let mut lines = Vec::new();\n\n    lines.push(format!(\n        \"Rules extracted from {} session(s) for {}\\n\",\n        output.sessions_analyzed,\n        output.project_path.display()\n    ));\n\n    let mut last_type = String::new();\n\n    for rule in &output.rules {\n        let (label, text) = match rule {\n            Rule::Correction(s) => (\"Correction\", s.as_str()),\n            Rule::Convention(s) => (\"Convention\", s.as_str()),\n            Rule::Context(s) => (\"Context\", s.as_str()),\n            Rule::Warning(s) => (\"Warning\", s.as_str()),\n        };\n\n        if label != last_type {\n            if !last_type.is_empty() {\n                lines.push(String::new());\n            }\n            lines.push(format!(\"  [{}]\", label));\n            last_type = label.to_string();\n        }\n\n        lines.push(format!(\"    - {}\", text));\n    }\n\n    lines.join(\"\\n\")\n}\n\n/// Extract rules and return them as inline text (for context command).\npub fn extract_rules_inline(data_dir: &Path, project_path: &Path) -> Result<String> {\n    let output = extract_rules(data_dir, project_path)?;\n\n    if output.rules.is_empty() {\n        return Ok(String::new());\n    }\n\n    // Use CLAUDE.md format as the inline representation\n    let content = format_claude(&output);\n\n after the comment\n        .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n        .join(\"\\n\");\n\n    Ok(without_header.trim().to_string())\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::event::Event;\n Debug)]\n17\t#[command(name = \"agentscribe\")]\n18\t#[command(author, version, about, long_about = None)]\n19\tstruct Args {\n20\t    #[command(subcommand)]\n21\t    command: Commands,\n22\t}\n23\t\n24\t/// Available CLI commands\n25\t#[derive(Subcommand, Debug)]\n26\t#[allow(clippy::large_enum_variant)]\n27\tenum Commands {\n28\t    /// Manage global config and data directory\n29\t    Config {\n30\t        #[command(subcommand)]\n31\t        action: ConfigAction,\n32\t    },\n33\t    /// Manage scraper plugin definitions\n34\t    Plugins {\n35\t        #[command(subcommand)]\n36\t        action: PluginsAction,\n37\t    },\n38\t    /// Discover and read agent log files\n39\t    Scrape {\n40\t        /// Plugin name to scrape (default: all)\n41\t        #[arg(short, long)]\n42\t        plugin: Option<String>,\n43\t\n44\t        /// Specific file to scrape\n45\t        #[arg(short, long)]\n46\t        file: Option<PathBuf>,\n47\t\n48\t        ///       output_events: bool,\n55\t\n56\t        /// JSON output\n57\t        #[arg(long)]\n58\t        json: bool,\n59\t    },\n60\t    /// Manage the Tantivy search index\n61\t    Index {\n62\t        #[command(subcommand)]\n63\t        action: IndexAction,\n64\t    },\n65\t    /// Show tracked agents and session counts\n66\t    Status {\n67\t        /// JSON output\n68\t        #[arg(long)]\n69\t        json: bool,\n70\t\n71\t        /// Show status for a specific plugin only\n72\t        #[arg(short, long)]\n73\t        plugin: Option<String>,\n74\t    },\n75\t    /// Query the Tantivy index for matching sessions\n76\t    Search {\n77\t        /// Search query string (Tantivy query syntax)\n78\t        query: Option<String>,\n79\t\n80\t        /// Error fingerprint pattern to search\n81\t        #[arg(long)]\n82\t        error: Option<String>,\n83\t\n84\t        /// Code content query\n85\t        #[arg(long)]\n86\t        code: Option<String>,\n87\t\n88\t        /// Language filter for code search\n89\t        #[arg(long)]\n90\t        lang: Option<String>,\n91\t\n92\t        /// Return only extracted solutions\n93\t        #[arg(long)]\n94\t        solution_only: bool,\n95\t\n96\t        /// Find sessions similar to this session ID\n97\t        #[arg(long)]\n98\t        like: Option<String>,\n99\t\n100\t        /// Retrieve a specific session by ID\n101\t        #[arg(long)]\n102\t        session: Option<String>,\n103\t\n104\t        /// Filter by source agent type (repeatable)\n105\t        #[arg(short, long)]\n106\t        agent: Vec<String>,\n107\t\n108\t        /// Filter by project path\n109\t        #[arg(long)]\n110\t        project: Option<String>,\n111\t\n112\t        /// Only match sessions after this timestamp\n113\t        #[arg(long)]\n114\t        since: Option<String>,\n115\t\n116\t        /// Only match sessions before this timestamp\n117\t        #[arg(long)]\n118\t        before: Option<String>,\n119\t\n120\t        /// Filter by tag (repeatable, AND logic)\n121\t        #[arg(short = 't', long)]\n122\t        tag: Vec<String>,\n123\t\n124\t        /// Filter by outcome (success, failure, abandoned, unknown)\n125\t        #[arg(long)]\n126\t        outcome: Option<String>,\n127\t\n128\t        /// Filter by doc type (session, code_artifact)\n129\t        #[arg(long)]\n130\t        r#type: Option<String>,\n131\t\n132\t        /// Filter by model name\n133\t        #[arg(long)]\n134\t        model: Option<String>,\n135\t\n136\t        /// Enable fuzzy matching on all query terms\n137\t        #[arg(long)]\n138\t        fuzzy: bool,\n139\t\n140\t        /// Levenshtein edit distance for fuzzy matching (overrides config; default: 1)\n141\t        #[arg(long)]\n142\t        edit_distance: Option<u8>,\n143\t\n144\t        /// Maximum number of results\n145\t        #[arg(short = 'n', long, default_value = \"10\")]\n146\t        max_results: usize,\n147\t\n148\t        /// Maximum snippet length per result (0 to omit)\n149\t        #[arg(long, default_value = \"200\")]\n150\t        snippet_length: usize,\n151\t\n152\t        /// Token budget for greedy knapsack context packing\n153\t        #[arg(long)]\n154\t        token_budget: Option<usize>,\n155\t\n156\t        /// Skip first N results (pagination)\n157\t        #[arg(long, default_value = \"0\")]\n158\t        offset: usize,\n159\t\n160\t        /// Sort order: relevance, newest, oldest, turns\n161\t        #[arg(short, long, default_value = \"relevance\")]\n162\t        sort: String,\n163\t\n164\t        /// Output a single-line hint (for shell hook integration)\n165\t        #[arg(long)]\n166\t        hint: bool,\n167\t\n168\t        /// JSON output\n169\t        #[arg(long)]\n170\t        json: bool,\n171\t    },\n172\t    /// Detect recurring problems from error fingerprints\n173\t    Recurring {\n174\t        /// Only consider sessions after this timestamp (ISO 8601, or relative like 30d, 12w)\n175\t        #[arg(long)]\n176\t        since: Option<String>,\n177\t\n178\t        /// Minimum occurrence count to report (default: 3)\n179\t        #[arg(long, {\n193\t        /// Project path to extract rules for\n194\t        project_path: PathBuf,\n195\t\n196\t        /// Output format: claude, cursor, or aider\n197\t        #[arg(long, value_enum, },\n204\t    /// Cross-agent performance comparison and analytics\n205\t    Analytics {\n206\t        /// Filter by agent name\n207\t        #[arg(short, long)]\n208\t        agent: (ISO 8601, or relative like 30d, 12w)\n215\t        #[arg(long)]\n216\t        since: Option<String>,\n217\t\n218\t        /// JSON structured output\n219\t        #[arg(long)]\n220\t        json: bool,\n221\t    },\n222\t    /// Garbage collect old session files and index entries\n223\t    Gc {\n224\t        /// Delete sessions older than this duration (e.g., 30d, 12w, 6mo)\n225\t        /// Default: value from config.toml scrape.max_session_age_days\n226\t        #[arg(long)]\n227\t        older_than: Option<String>,\n228\t\n229\t        /// Show what would be deleted without acting\n230\t        #[arg(long)]\n231\t        dry_run: bool,\n232\t\n233\t        /// JSON output\n234\t        #[arg(long)]\n235\t        json: bool,\n236\t    },\n237\t    /// Bidirectional git commit ↔ session linking\n238\t    Blame {\n239\t        /// File path with optional line number (e.g., src/auth.rs or src/auth.rs:42)\n240\t        spec: String,\n241\t\n242\t        /// Maximum        json: bool,\n249\t    },\n250\t    /// Show all sessions that touched a file (chronological)\n251\t    File {\n252\t        /// File path or glob pattern (e.g., src/auth.rs or \"src/auth/**\")\n253\t        path: String,\n254\t\n255\t        /// Maximum number of results (default: 50)\n256\t        #[arg(short = 'n', long, default_value = \"50\")]\n257\t        max_results: /// Show summary for a specific session ID\n266\t        #[arg(long)]\n267\t        session: Option<String>,\n268\t\n269\t        /// Only include sessions after this timestamp (ISO 8601, or relative like 7d)\n270\t        #[arg(long)]\n271\t        since: Option<String>,\n272\t\n273\t        /// Filter by project path\n274\t        #[arg(long)]\n275\t        project: Option<String>,\n276\t\n277\t        /// Maximum number of sessions to summarize (default: 20)\n278\t        #[arg(short = 'n', long, default_value = \"20\")]\n279\t        max_results: usize,\n280\t\n281\t        /// Write summaries to .md files alongside session JSONL files\n282\t        #[arg(long)]\n283\t        write: bool,\n284\t\n285\t        /// JSON output\n286\t        #[arg(long)]\n287\t        json: shell: String,\n293\t    },\n294\t    /// Show known gotchas, error patterns, and statistics for a file\n295\t    FileKnowledge {\n296\t        /// File path to analyze\n297\t        file_path: Only include sessions after this timestamp (ISO 8601, or relative like 7d, 30d)\n306\t        #[arg(long, default_value = \"7d\")]\n307\t        since: String,\n308\t\n309\t        /// Write output to file instead of stdout\n310\t        #[arg(short, long)]\n311\t        output: Option<String>,\n312\t\n313\t        /// JSON structured output\n314\t        #[arg(long)]\n315\t        json: bool,\n316\t    },\n317\t    /// Generate a quarterly Pulse Report (State of AI Coding)\n318\t    PulseReport {\n319\t        /// Quarter to report on: YYYY-Q1 through YYYY-Q4, or \"current\" (default: current)\n320\t        #[arg(long, default_value = \"current\")]\n321\t        quarter: String,\n322\t\n323\t        /// Write output to file instead of stdout\n324\t        #[arg(short, String,\n330\t    },\n331\t    /// Show per-account Claude Code utilization (5h and 7d rolling windows)\n332\t    Capacity {\n333\t        /// Claude config directories to scan, one per account (default: ~/.claude + auto-discovered ~/.claude-* dirs)\n334\t        #[arg(long)]\n335\t        account_dir: Vec<PathBuf>,\n336\t\n337\t        /// Maximum age of cached usage data in seconds before falling back to JSONL (default: 600)\n338\t        #[arg(long, default_value = \"600\")]\n339\t        cache_max_age: u64,\n340\t\n341\t        /// JSON output\n342\t        #[arg(long)]\n343\t        json: bool,\n344\t    },\n345\t    /// Generate shell completion script\n346\t    Completions {\n347\t        /// Shell to generate completions for\n348\t        shell: Shell,\n349\t    },\n350\t    /// Transcribe an audio file using the configured local Whisper model\n351\t    Transcribe {\n352\t        /// Audio file to transcribe (wav, mp3, or m4a)\n353\t        input: PathBuf,\n354\t\n355\t        /// Wait for the job to complete and print the transcript (default: true)\n356\t        #[arg(long, default_value_t = true)]\n357\t        wait: bool,\n358\t\n359\t        /// Maximum seconds to wait for completion (default: 600)\n360\t        #[arg(long, default_value_t = 600)]\n361\t        timeout: u64,\n362\t\n363\t        /// JSON output (includes timestamps and metadata)\n364\t        #[arg(long)]\n365\t        json: bool,\n366\t    },\n367\t    /// Pre-task priming query for agent workers\n368\t    Context {\n369\t        /// Task description to query for context\n370\t        query: String,\n371\t\n372\t        /// Token budget for context packing (default: 3000)\n373\t        #[arg(long, default_value = \"3000\")]\n374\t        token_budget: usize,\n375\t\n376\t        /// Scope rules extraction to this project path\n377\t        #[arg(long)]\n378\t        project: Option<PathBuf>,\n379\t\n380\t        /// JSON output (wrap sections in JSON object)\n381\t        #[arg(long)]\n382\t        json: bool,\n383\t    },\n384\t}\n385\t\n386\t/// Config subcommands\n387\t#[derive(Subcommand, Debug)]\n388\tenum ConfigAction {\n389\t    /// Initialize global config and data directory\n390\t    Init {\n391\t        /// Force reinitialization\n392\t        #[arg(long)]\n393\t        force: bool,\n394\t    },\n395\t    /// Show current configuration\n396\t    Show,\n397\t    /// Set a configuration value\n398\t    Set {\n399\t        /// Configuration key (e.g., general.log_level)\n400\t        key: String,\n401\t        /// Configuration value\n402\t        value: String,\n403\t    },\n404\t    /// Get a configuration value\n405\t    Get {\n406\t        /// Configuration key\n407\t        key: String,\n408\t    },\n409\t}\n410\t\n411\t/// Plugins subcommands\n412\t#[derive(Subcommand, Debug)]\n413\tenum PluginsAction {\n414\t    /// List available plugins\n415\t    List {\n416\t        /// Show detailed information\n417\t        #[arg(long)]\n418\t        verbose: bool,\n419\t    },\n420\t    /// Validate a plugin definition\n421\t    Validate {\n422\t        /// Path to plugin file\n423\t        path: PathBuf,\n424\t    },\n425\t    /// Show plugin details\n426\t    Show {\n427\t        /// Plugin name\n428\t        name: String,\n429\t    },\n430\t}\n431\t\n432\t/// Daemon subcommands\n433\t#[derive(Subcommand, Debug)]\n434\tenum IndexAction {\n435\t    /// Drop and rebuild the index from session files\n436\t    Rebuild {\n437\t        /// Only re-index sessions from this plugin\n438\t        #[arg(short, long)]\n439\t        plugin: Option<String>,\n440\t\n441\t        /// Tantivy writer heap size in MB\n442\t        #[arg(long, default_value = \"50\")]\n443\t        heap_size: usize,\n444\t    },\n445\t    /// Show index statistics\n446\t    Stats,\n447\t    /// Merge segments for better query performance\n448\t    Optimize,\n449\t}\n450\t\n451\t/// Daemon subcommands\n452\t#[derive(Subcommand, Debug)]\n453\tenum DaemonAction {\n454\t    /// Start the daemon in the background\n455\t    Start,\n456\t    /// Run the daemon in the foreground (for systemd)\n457\t    Run,\n458\t    /// Stop a running daemon\n459\t    Stop,\n460\t    /// Show daemon status (PID, uptime, RSS, activity)\n461\t    Status {\n462\t        /// JSON output\n463\t        #[arg(long)]\n464\t        json: bool,\n465\t    },\n466\t    /// Tail the daemon log\n467\t    Logs {\n468\t        /// Follow mode (like tail -f)\n469\t        #[arg(short = 'f', long)]\n470\t        follow: bool,\n471\t\n472\t        /// Number of lines to show (default: 20)\n473\t        #[arg(short = 'n', long, default_value = \"20\")]\n474\t        lines: usize,\n475\t    },\n476\t    /// Install systemd user service unit (~/.config/systemd/user/agentscribe.service)\n477\t    Install,\n478\t    /// Remove systemd user service unit\n479\t    Uninstall,\n480\t}\n481\t\n482\t/// Table row for plugin list output\n483\t#[derive(Tabled)]\n484\tstruct PluginRow {\n485\t    name: String,\n486\t    version: String,\n487\t    format: String,\n488\t}\n489\t\n490\t/// Run the CLI\n491\tpub fn run() -> Result<()> {\n492\t    let args = Args::parse();\n493\t\n494\t    match args.command {\n495\t        Commands::Config { action } => run_config(action),\n496\t        Commands::Plugins { action } => run_plugins(action),\n497\t        Commands::Scrape {\n498\t            plugin,\n499\t            file,\n500\t            dry_run,\n501\t            output_events,\n502\t            json,\n503\t        } => run_scrape(plugin, file, dry_run, output_events, json),\n504\t        Commands::Status { json, plugin } => run_status(json, plugin),\n505\t        Commands::Index { action } => run_index(action),\n506\t        Commands::Search {\n507\t            query,\n508\t            error,\n509\t            code,\n510\t            lang,\n511\t            solution_only,\n512\t            like,\n513\t            session,\n514\t            agent,\n515\t            project,\n516\t            since,\n517\t            before,\n518\t            tag,\n519\t            outcome,\n520\t            r#type,\n521\t            model,\n522\t            fuzzy,\n523\t            edit_distance,\n524\t            max_results,\n525\t            snippet_length,\n526\t            token_budget,\n527\t            offset,\n528\t            sort,\n529\t            hint,\n530\t            json,\n531\t        } => run_search(\n532\t            query,\n533\t            error,\n534\t            code,\n535\t            lang,\n536\t            solution_only,\n537\t            like,\n538\t            session,\n539\t            agent,\n540\t            project,\n541\t            since,\n542\t            before,\n543\t            tag,\n544\t            outcome,\n545\t            r#type,\n546\t            model,\n547\t            fuzzy,\n548\t            edit_distance,\n549\t            max_results,\n550\t            snippet_length,\n551\t            token_budget,\n552\t            offset,\n553\t            sort,\n554\t            hint,\n555\t            json,\n556\t        ),\n557\t        Commands::Recurring {\n558\t            since,\n559\t            threshold,\n560\t            json,\n561\t        } => run_recurring(since, threshold, json),\n562\t        Commands::Daemon { action } => run_daemon(action),\n563\t        Commands::Rules {\n564\t            project_path,\n565\t            format,\n566\t            json,\n567\t        } => run_rules(project_path, format, json),\n568\t        Commands::Analytics {\n569\t            agent,\n570\t            project,\n571\t            since,\n572\t            json,\n573\t        } => run_analytics(agent, project, since, json),\n574\t        Commands::Gc {\n575\t            older_than,\n576\t            dry_run,\n577\t            json,\n578\t        } => run_gc(older_than, dry_run, json),\n579\t        Commands::Blame {\n580\t            spec,\n581\t            max_results,\n582\t            json,\n583\t        } => run_blame(spec, max_results, json),\n584\t        Commands::File {\n585\t            path,\n586\t            max_results,\n587\t            json,\n588\t        } => run_file(path, max_results, json),\n589\t        Commands::Summarize {\n590\t            session,\n591\t            since,\n592\t            project,\n593\t            max_results,\n594\t            write,\n595\t            json,\n596\t        } => run_summarize(session, since, project, max_results, write, json),\n597\t        Commands::ShellHook { shell } => run_shell_hook(&shell),\n598\t        Commands::FileKnowledge { file_path, json } => run_file_knowledge(&file_path, json),\n599\t        Commands::Digest {\n600\t            since,\n601\t            output,\n602\t            json,\n603\t        } => run_digest(since, output, json),\n604\t        Commands::PulseReport {\n605\t            quarter,\n606\t            output,\n607\t            format,\n608\t        } => run_pulse_report(quarter, output, format),\n609\t        Commands::Capacity {\n610\t            account_dir,\n611\t            cache_max_age,\n612\t            json,\n613\t        } => run_capacity(account_dir, cache_max_age, json),\n614\t        Commands::Transcribe {\n615\t            input,\n616\t            wait,\n617\t            timeout,\n618\t            json,\n619\t        } => run_transcribe(input, wait, timeout, json),\n620\t        Commands::Context {\n621\t            query,\n622\t            token_budget,\n623\t            project,\n624\t            json,\n625\t        } => run_context(query, token_budget, project, json),\n626\t        Commands::Completions { shell } => {\n627\t            let mut cmd = Args::command();\n628\t            generate(shell, &mut cmd, \"agentscribe\", &mut io::stdout());\n629\t            Ok(())\n630\t        }\n631\t    }\n632\t}\n633\t\n634\t/// Run daemon commands\n635\tfn run_daemon(action: DaemonAction) -> Result<()> {\n636\t    // install/uninstall don't need the data directory to exist\n637\t    match action {\n638\t        DaemonAction::Install => return crate::daemon::install_service(),\n639\t        DaemonAction::Uninstall => return crate::daemon::uninstall_service(),\n640\t        _ => {}\n641\t    }\n642\t\n643\t    let config = load_config()?;\n644\t    let data_dir = config.data_dir()?;\n645\t\n646\t    // Ensure data directory exists for all other daemon commands\n647\t    if !data_dir.exists() {\n648\t        eprintln!(\"AgentScribe not initialized. Run 'agentscribe config init' to set up.\");\n649\t        std::process::exit(1);\n650\t    }\n651\t\n652\t    match action {\n653\t        DaemonAction::Start => {\n654\t            crate::daemon::start(&data_dir)?;\n655\t            Ok(())\n656\t        }\n657\t        DaemonAction::Run => crate::daemon::run(&data_dir),\n658\t        DaemonAction::Stop => crate::daemon::stop(&data_dir),\n659\t        DaemonAction::Status { json } => {\n660\t            let info = crate::daemon::status(&data_dir)?;\n661\t            if json {\n662\t                println!(\"{}\", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&info).unwrap());\n663\t            } else {\n664\t                print_daemon_status(&info);\n665\t            }\n666\t            Ok(())\n667\t        }\n668\t        DaemonAction::Logs { follow, lines } => crate::daemon::logs(&data_dir, follow, lines),\n669\t        // Already handled above\n670\t        DaemonAction::Install | DaemonAction::Uninstall => unreachable!(),\n671\t    }\n672\t}\n673\t\n674\t/// Format daemon status for human-readable output\n675\tfn print_daemon_status(info: &crate::daemon::DaemonInfo) {\n676\t    if !info.running {\n677\t        println!(\"Daemon: stopped\");\n678\t        return;\n679\t    }\n680\t\n681\t    println!(\"Daemon: running\");\n682\t\n683\t    if let Some(pid) = info.pid {\n684\t        println!(\"  PID: {}\", pid);\n685\t    }\n686\t\n687\t    if let Some(secs) = info.uptime_secs {\n688\t        println!(\"  Uptime: {}\", crate::daemon::format_duration(secs));\n689\t    }\n690\t\n691\t    if let Some(rss) = info.rss_bytes {\n692\t        println!(\"  RSS: {}\", crate::daemon::format_bytes(rss));\n693\t    }\n694\t\n695\t    if let Some(peak) = info.peak_rss_bytes {\n696\t        println!(\"  Peak RSS: {}\", crate::daemon::format_bytes(peak));\n697\t    }\n698\t\n699\t    if let Some(sessions) = info.sessions_indexed {\n700\t        println!(\"  Sessions indexed: {}\", sessions);\n701\t    }\n702\t\n703\t    if let Some(ts) = info.last_scrape {\n704\t        println!(\"  Last scrape: {}\", ts.to_rfc3339());\n705\t    }\n706\t\n707\t    if let Some(ts) = info.started_at {\n708\t        println!(\"  Started at: {}\", ts.to_rfc3339());\n709\t    }\n710\t}\n711\t\n712\t/// Run config commands\n713\tfn run_config(action: ConfigAction) -> Result<()> {\n714\t    match action {\n715\t        ConfigAction::Init { force } => {\n716\t            let data_dir = config::init(force)?;\n717\t            println!(\n718\t                \"Initialized config.search.default_snippet_length\n743\t            );\n744\t            Ok(())\n745\t        }\n746\t        ConfigAction::Set { key, value } => {\n747\t            let mut config = value.parse().unwrap_or(0);\n756\t                }\n757\t                [\"scrape\", \"git_auto_commit\"] => {\n758\t                    config.scrape.git_auto_commit =\n759\t                        matches!(value.to_lowercase().as_str(), \"true\" | \"1\" | \"yes\");\n760\t                }\n761\t                _ => {\n762\t                    eprintln!(\"Unknown configuration key: {}\", key);\n763\t                    return Ok(());\n764\t                }\n765\t            }\n766\t\n767\t            let data_dir = config.data_dir()?;\n768\t            let config_path = data_dir.join(\"config.toml\");\n769\t            config.save(&config_path)?;\n770\t            println!(\"Set {} = {}\", key, value);\n771\t            Ok(())\n772\t        }\n773\t        ConfigAction::Get { key } => {\n774\t            let config = load_config()?;\n775\t            let value = match key.split('.').collect::<Vec<_>>().as_slice() {\n776\t                [\"general\", \"data_dir\"] => config.data_dir()?.display().to_string(),\n777\t                [\"general\", \"log_level\"] => config.general.log_level.clone(),\n778\t                [\"scrape\", config.scrape.max_session_age_days.to_string()\n781\t                }\n782\t                [\"scrape\", \"git_auto_commit\"] => config.scrape.git_auto_commit.to_string(),\n783\t                _ => {\n784\t                    eprintln!(\"Unknown configuration key: {}\", key);\n785\t                    return Ok(());\n786\t                }\n787\t            };\n788\t            println!(\"{}\", value);\n789\t            Ok(())\n790\t        }\n791\t    }\n792\t}\n793\t\n794\t/// Run plugins commands\n795\tfn run_plugins(action: PluginsAction) -> Result<()> {\n796\t    match action {\n797\t        PluginsAction::List { verbose } => {\n798\t            let config = load_config()?;\n799\t            let data_dir = config.data_dir()?;\n800\t            let plugin_dir = run_scrape(\n887\t    plugin: Option<String>,\n888\t    file: Option<PathBuf>,\n889\t    dry_run: bool,\n890\t    _output_events: bool,\n891\t    _json: bool,\n892\t) -> Result<()> {\n893\t    let Use --plugin to specify.\");\n924\t                std::process::exit(1);\n925\t            }\n926\t        });\n927\t\n928\t        if let Some(p) = scraper.plugin_manager().get(&plugin_name).cloned() {\n929\t            if dry_run {\n930\t                println!(\"Dry run - would scrape with plugin: {}\", plugin_name);\n931\t         println!(\"  Would detect sessions and parse events...\");\n935\t            } else {\n936\t                let result = scraper.scrape_file(&file_path, &p)?;\n937\t                scraper.state_manager().save()?;\n938\t                println!(\"Scraped {} session(s)\", result.sessions_scraped);\n939\t                if result.sessions_indexed > 0 {\n940\t                    println!(\"Indexed {} session(s)\", result.sessions_indexed);\n941\t                }\n942\t                if !result.errors.is_empty() {\n943\t                    eprintln!(\"Errors: {}\", std::process::exit(1);\n952\t        }\n953\t    } else if let Some(plugin_name) = plugin {\n954\t        // Scrape specific plugin\n955\t        println!(\"Scraping plugin: {}\", plugin_name);\n956\t\n957\t        if let Some(p) = scraper.plugin_manager().get(&plugin_name).cloned() {\n958\t            if dry_run {\n959\t                println!(\"Dry run - would discover and         }\n964\t            } else {\n965\t                let result = scraper.scrape_plugin(&p)?;\n966\t                scraper.state_manager().save()?;\n967\t                println!(\n968\t                    \"Scraped {} session(s) from {} file(s)\",\n969\t                    result.sessions_scraped, result.files_processed\n970\t                );\n971\t                if result.sessions_indexed > 0 {\n972\t                    println!(\"Indexed {} session(s)\", result.sessions_indexed);\n973\t                }\n974\t                if result.files_skipped > 0 {\n975\t                    println!(\"  ({} files unchanged, skipped)\", result.files_skipped);\n976\t                }\n977\t                if !result.errors.is_empty() {\n978\t                    eprintln!(\"Errors: {}\", result.errors.len());\n979\t                    for error in &result.errors[..result.errors.len().min(5)] {\n980\t                        eprintln!(\"  - {}: {}\", error.file, error.message);\n981\t                    }\n982\t                    if result.errors.len() > 5 {\n983\t                        eprintln!(\"  ... and {} more\", result.errors.len() - 5);\n984\t                    }\n985\t                }\n986\t                if config.scrape.git_auto_commit && git_auto_commit(&data_dir, &result)? {\n987\t                    println!(\"Git:       std::process::exit(1);\n993\t        }\n994\t    } else {\n995\t        // Scrape all plugins\n996\t        println!(\"Scraping all plugins...\");\n997\t\n998\t        if dry_run {\n999\t            for plugin_name in scraper.plugin_manager().names() {\n1000\t                println!(\"  - {}\", plugin_name);\n1001\t            }\n1002\t        } else {\n1003\t            let result = scraper.scrape_all()?;\n1004\t            println!(\"Scraped {} session(s) total\", result.sessions_scraped);\n1005\t            if result.sessions_indexed > 0 {\n1006\t                println!(\"Indexed {} session(s)\", result.sessions_indexed);\n1007\t            }\n1008\t            if !result.errors.is_empty() {\n1009\t                eprintln!(\"Errors: {}\", result.errors.len());\n1010\t            }\n1011\t            if config.scrape.git_auto_commit && git_auto_commit(&data_dir, &result)? {\n1012\t                println!(\n1013\t }\n1018\t        }\n1019\t    }\n1020\t\n1021\t    Ok(())\n1022\t}\n1023\t\n1024\t/// Run search command\n1025\t#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]\n1026\tfn run_search(\n1027\t    query: Option<String>,\n1028\t    error: Option<String>,\n1029\t    code: Option<String>,\n1030\t    lang: Option<String>,\n1031\t    solution_only: bool,\n1032\t    like: Option<String>,\n1033\t    session: Option<String>,\n1034\t    agent: Vec<String>,\n1035\t    project: Option<String>,\n1036\t    since: Option<String>,\n1037\t    before: Option<String>,\n1038\t    tag: Vec<String>,\n1039\t    outcome: Option<String>,\n1040\t    doc_type_filter: Option<String>,\n1041\t    model: Option<String>,\n1042\t    fuzzy: bool,\n1043\t    edit_distance: Option<u8>,\n1044\t    max_results: usize,\n1045\t    snippet_length: usize,\n1046\t    token_budget: Option<usize>,\n1047\t    offset: usize,\n1048\t    sort: String,\n1049\t    hint: bool,\n1050\t    json: bool,\n1051\t) -> Result<()> {\n1052\t    let config = load_config()?;\n1053\t    let data_dir = config.data_dir()?;\n1054\t\n1055\t    let sort_order = match sort.as_str() {\n1056\t        \"newest\" => SortOrder::Newest,\n1057\t        \"oldest\" => SortOrder::Oldest,\n1058\t        \"turns\" => SortOrder::Turns,\n1059\t        _ => SortOrder::Relevance,\n1060\t    };\n1061\t\n1062\t    let since_dt = since.as_deref().map(search::parse_datetime).transpose()?;\n1063\t\n1064\t    let before_dt = before.as_deref().map(search::parse_datetime).transpose()?;\n1065\t\n1066\t    let opts = SearchOptions {\n1067\t        query,\n1068\t        error_pattern: error,\n1069\t        code_query: code,\n1070\t        code_lang: lang,\n1071\t        solution_only,\n1072\t        like_session: like,\n1073\t        session_id: session,\n1074\t        agent,\n1075\t        project,\n1076\t        since: since_dt,\n1077\t        before: before_dt,\n1078\t        tag,\n1079\t        outcome,\n1080\t        doc_type_filter,\n1081\t        model,\n1082\t        file_path: None,\n1083\t        git_commit: None,\n1084\t        fuzzy,\n1085\t        fuzzy_distance:
