}\n699\t}\n700\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"529679b9-8236-48d2-991d-618f3bcacbf5","uuid":"79210718-1f73-4048-b632-cad10fd1069b","timestamp":"2026-05-01T13:03:24.686Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/AgentScribe/src/file_knowledge.rs","content":"//! File knowledge map: aggregates known gotchas, error patterns, and statistics\n//! for a specific file path across all sessions.\n//!\n//! Combines Tantivy index data with anti-pattern sidecar files to produce\n//! a comprehensive view of what's known about a file.\n\nuse crate::analytics;\nuse crate::config::Config;\nuse crate::error::Result;\nuse crate::index::build_schema;\nuse crate::search::open_index;\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\nuse serde::Serialize;\nuse std::collections::HashMap;\nuse std::path::Path;\n\n/// A known gotcha extracted from anti-pattern data for a specific file.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]\npub struct KnownGotcha {\n    /// Description of the anti-pattern or issue\n    pub pattern: String,\n    /// Error fingerprints associated with this gotcha\n    pub error_fingerprints: Vec<String>,\n    /// Sessions where this gotcha was observed\n    pub affected_sessions: Vec<String>,\n    /// Sessions that successfully resolved this issue (if any)\n    pub resolution_sessions: Vec<String>,\n}\n\n/// Error pattern summary for a file.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]\npub struct FileErrorPattern {\n    /// Normalized error fingerprint\n    pub fingerprint: String,\n    /// Number of sessions where this error appeared for this file\n    pub session_count: usize,\n    /// Last time this error was seen\n    pub last_seen: DateTime<Utc>,\n}\n\n/// File knowledge map output.\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize)]\npub struct FileKnowledge {\n    /// The file path being analyzed\n    pub file_path: String,\n    /// Number of sessions that touched this file\n    pub session_count: usize,\n    /// Success rate across sessions touching this file\n    pub success_rate: f64,\n    /// Breakdown of problem types\n    pub problem_types: HashMap<String, usize>,\n    /// Known gotchas from anti-pattern data\n    pub gotchas: Vec<KnownGotcha>,\n    /// Common error patterns\n    pub error_patterns: Vec<FileErrorPattern>,\n    /// Agents that have worked on this file\n    pub agents: Vec<String>,\n    /// Projects where this file appears\n    pub projects: Vec<String>,\n    /// Session IDs for reference\n    pub sample_sessions: Vec<String>,\n}\n\n/// Build a file knowledge map for a specific file path.\npub fn build_file_knowledge(\n    data_dir: &Path,\n    file_path: &str,\n    _config: &Config,\n) -> Result<FileKnowledge> {\n    let sessions = find_sessions_for_file(data_dir, file_path)?;\n\n    if sessions.is_empty() {\n        return Ok(FileKnowledge {\n            file_path: file_path.to_string(),\n            session_count: 0,\n            success_rate: 0.0,\n            problem_types: HashMap::new(),\n            gotchas: vec![],\n            error_patterns: vec![],\n            agents: vec![],\n            projects: vec![],\n            sample_sessions: vec![],\n        });\n    }\n\n    // Step 2: Compute analytics for these sessions\n    let session_ids: Vec<String> = sessions.iter().map(|s| s.session_id.clone()).collect();\n\n    // Step 3: Aggregate statistics\n    let total = sessions.len();\n    let successes = sessions\n        .iter()\n        .filter(|s| s.outcome.as_deref() == Some(\"success\"))\n        .count();\n    let success_rate = if total > 0 {\n        successes as f64 / total as f64 * 100.0\n    } else {\n        0.0\n    };\n\n    let mut problem_types: HashMap<String, usize> = HashMap::new();\n    let mut agents: Vec<String> = sessions\n        .iter()\n        .map(|s| s.source_agent.clone())\n        .collect::<std::collections::HashSet<_>>()\n        .into_iter()\n        .collect();\n    agents.sort();\n\n    let mut projects: Vec<String> = sessions\n        .iter()\n        .filter_map(|s| s.project.clone())\n        .collect::<std::collections::HashSet<_>>()\n        .into_iter()\n        .collect();\n    projects.sort();\n\n    for session in &sessions {\n        *problem_types\n            .entry(session.primary_type.as_str().to_string())\n            .or_insert(0) += 1;\n    }\n\n    // Step 4: Collect error patterns\n    let mut error_map: HashMap<String, (usize, DateTime<Utc>)> = HashMap::new();\n    for session in &sessions {\n        for fp in &session.error_fingerprints {\n            let entry = error_map\n                .entry(fp.clone())\n                .or_insert((0, session.timestamp));\n            entry.0 += 1;\n            if session.timestamp > entry.1 {\n                entry.1 = session.timestamp;\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    let mut error_patterns: Vec<FileErrorPattern> = error_map\n        .into_iter()\n        .map(|(fingerprint, (count, last_seen))| FileErrorPattern {\n            fingerprint,\n            session_count: count,\n            last_seen,\n        })\n        .collect();\n    error_patterns.sort_by_key(|b| std::cmp::Reverse(b.session_count));\n\n    // Step 5: Load anti-pattern gotchas from sidecar files\n    let gotchas = load_gotchas_for_sessions(data_dir, &session_ids)?;\n\n    // Step 6: Sample sessions (last 5 by timestamp)\n    let mut sorted_sessions = sessions;\n    sorted_sessions.sort_by_key(|b| std::cmp::Reverse(b.timestamp));\n    let sample_sessions: Vec<String> = sorted_sessions\n        .iter()\n        .take(5)\n        .map(|s| s.session_id.clone())\n        .collect();\n\n    Ok(FileKnowledge {\n        file_path: file_path.to_string(),\n        session_count: total,\n        success_rate,\n        problem_types,\n        gotchas,\n        error_patterns,\n        agents,\n        projects,\n        sample_sessions,\n    })\n}\n\n/// Session data for file knowledge extraction.\nstruct FileSessionData {\n    session_id: String,\n    source_agent: String,\n    project: Option<String>,\n    timestamp: DateTime<Utc>,\n    outcome: Option<String>,\n    error_fingerprints: Vec<String>,\n    primary_type: analytics::ProblemType,\n}\n\n/// Find all sessions that touch a specific file path.\nfn find_sessions_for_file(data_dir: &Path, file_path: &str) -> Result<Vec<FileSessionData>> {\n    let index = open_index(data_dir)?;\n    let reader = index.reader().map_err(|e| {\n        crate::error::AgentScribeError::DataDir(format!(\"Failed to create index reader: {}\", e))\n    })?;\n    let searcher = reader.searcher();\n    let total_docs = searcher.num_docs();\n\n    if total_docs == 0 {\n        return Ok(vec![]);\n    }\n\n    let (_schema, fields) = build_schema();\n\n    let mut sessions = Vec::new();\n\n    use tantivy::collector::TopDocs;\n    use tantivy::query::BooleanQuery;\n    use tantivy::query::TermQuery;\n    use tantivy::schema::IndexRecordOption;\n\n    let fp_field = fields.file_paths;\n\n    // Build a term query for the file path\n    let term = tantivy::schema::Term::from_field_text(fp_field, file_path);\n    let fp_query: Box<dyn tantivy::query::Query> =\n        Box::new(TermQuery::new(term, IndexRecordOption::Basic));\n\n    // Combine with doc_type=session filter\n    let dt_field = fields.doc_type;\n    let dt_term = tantivy::schema::Term::from_field_text(dt_field, \"session\");\n    let dt_query: Box<dyn tantivy::query::Query> =\n        Box::new(TermQuery::new(dt_term, IndexRecordOption::Basic));\n\n    let combined = BooleanQuery::intersection(vec![fp_query, dt_query]);\n\n    let all_docs: Vec<_> = searcher\n        .search(&combined, &TopDocs::with_limit(total_docs as usize))\n        .map_err(|e| crate::error::AgentScribeError::DataDir(format!(\"Search failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n    for (_score, doc_addr) in all_docs {\n        if let Some(data) = analytics::extract_session_data(&searcher, doc_addr, &fields) {\n            sessions.push(FileSessionData {\n                session_id: data.session_id,\n                source_agent: data.source_agent,\n                project: data.project,\n                timestamp: data.timestamp,\n                outcome: data.outcome,\n                error_fingerprints: data.error_fingerprints,\n                primary_type: data.primary_type,\n            });\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(sessions)\n}\n\n/// Load gotchas from anti-pattern sidecar files for the given sessions.\nfn load_gotchas_for_sessions(data_dir: &Path, session_ids: &[String]) -> Result<Vec<KnownGotcha>> {\n    let sessions_dir = data_dir.join(\"sessions\");\n    let mut gotchas = Vec::new();\n\n    for session_id in session_ids {\n        let parts: Vec<&str> = session_id.splitn(2, '/').collect();\n        if parts.len() != 2 {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        let sidecar_path = sessions_dir\n            .join(parts[0])\n            .join(format!(\"{}.anti-patterns.jsonl\", parts[1]));\n\n        if !sidecar_path.exists() {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        let content = match std::fs::read_to_string(&sidecar_path) {\n            Ok(c) => c,\n            Err(_) => continue,\n        };\n\n        for line in content.lines() {\n            if line.trim().is_empty() {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            let pattern: crate::enrichment::antipatterns::AntiPattern =\n                match serde_json::from_str(line) {\n                    Ok(p) => p,\n                    Err(_) => continue,\n                };\n\n            gotchas.push(KnownGotcha {\n                pattern: pattern.pattern,\n                error_fingerprints: pattern.error_fingerprints,\n                affected_sessions: pattern.session_ids,\n                resolution_sessions: pattern.alternative_session_ids,\n            });\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Deduplicate by pattern description\n    let mut seen = std::collections::HashSet::new();\n    gotchas.retain(|g| seen.insert(g.pattern.clone()));\n\n    Ok(gotchas)\n}\n\n/// Format file knowledge as human-readable output.\npub fn format_human(knowledge: &FileKnowledge) -> String {\n    let mut lines = Vec::new();\n\n    lines.push(format!(\"File Knowledge: {}\\n\", knowledge.file_path));\n\n    if knowledge.session_count == 0 {\n        lines.push(\"No sessions found touching this file.\".to_string());\n        return lines.join(\"\\n\");\n    }\n\n    // Overview\n    lines.push(format!(\n        \"  {} session(s) | {:.0}% success rate\",\n        knowledge.session_count, knowledge.success_rate,\n    ));\n\n    if !knowledge.agents.is_empty() {\n        lines.push(format!(\"  Agents: {}\", knowledge.agents.join(\", \")));\n    }\n\n    if !knowledge.problem_types.is_empty() {\n        let mut types: Vec<_> = knowledge.problem_types.iter().collect();\n        types.sort_by(|a, b| b.1.cmp(a.1));\n        let type_str: Vec<String> = types.iter().map(|(t, c)| format!(\"{}:{}\", t, c)).collect();\n        lines.push(format!(\"  Problem types: {}\", type_str.join(\", \")));\n    }\n\n    lines.push(String::new());\n\n    // Known gotchas\n    if !knowledge.gotchas.is_empty() {\n        lines.push(\"Known Gotchas\".to_string());\n        lines.push(\"-------------\".to_string());\n        for (i, gotcha) in knowledge.gotchas.iter().enumerate() {\n            lines.push(format!(\"{}. {}\", i + 1, gotcha.pattern));\n            if !gotcha.error_fingerprints.is_empty() {\n                let fps: Vec<&str> = gotcha\n                    .error_fingerprints\n                    .iter()\n                    .map(|s| s.as_str())\n                    .take(3)\n                    .collect();\n                lines.push(format!(\"   Errors: {}\", fps.join(\", \")));\n            }\n            if !gotcha.resolution_sessions.is_empty() {\n                lines.push(format!(\n                    \"   Resolved in: {} session(s)\",\n                    gotcha.resolution_sessions.len()\n                ));\n            }\n        }\n        lines.push(String::new());\n    }\n\n    // Common error patterns\n    if !knowledge.error_patterns.is_empty() {\n        lines.push(\"Common Error Patterns\".to_string());\n        lines.push(\"---------------------\".to_string());\n        for ep in knowledge.error_patterns.iter().take(10) {\n            let fp_display = truncate_fingerprint(&ep.fingerprint, 80);\n            lines.push(format!(\n                \"  {} ({} session(s), last: {})\",\n                fp_display,\n                ep.session_count,\n                ep.last_seen.format(\"%Y-%m-%d\"),\n            ));\n        }\n        lines.push(String::new());\n    }\n\n    // Sample sessions\n    if !knowledge.sample_sessions.is_empty() {\n        lines.push(\"Recent Sessions\".to_string());\n        lines.push(\"---------------\".to_string());\n        for sid in &knowledge.sample_sessions {\n            lines.push(format!(\"  - {}\", sid));\n        }\n    }\n\n    lines.join(\"\\n\")\n}\n\n/// Truncate a fingerprint for display.\nfn truncate_fingerprint(fp: &str, max_len: usize) -> String {\n    if fp.len() <= max_len {\n        return fp.to_string();\n    }\n    if let Some(colon_pos) = fp.find(':') {\n        let prefix = &fp[..=colon_pos];\n        let remaining = max_len.saturating_sub(prefix.len() + 4);\n        if remaining > 10 {\n            return format!(\n                \"{}{}...\",\n                prefix,\n                &fp[colon_pos + 1..colon_pos + 1 + remaining]\n            );\n        }\n    }\n    format!(\"{}...\", &fp[..max_len.saturating_sub(3)])\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_truncate_fingerprint() {\n        assert_eq!(\n            truncate_fingerprint(\"ErrorType:short\", 80),\n            \"ErrorType:short\"\n        );\n        assert!(truncate_fingerprint(\n            \"SomeVeryLongErrorType:this is a very long message that should be truncated\",\n            40\n        )\n        .ends_with(\"...\"));\n    }\n\n sample_sessions: vec![],\n        };\n\n        let output = format_human(&knowledge);\n        assert!(output.contains(\"src/main.rs\"));\n        assert!(output.contains(\"No sessions found\"));\n opts.max_results * 5\n174\t    } else {\n175\t        opts.max_results + opts.offset\n176\t    };\n177\t\n178\t    // Execute search\n179\t    let mut top_docs: Vec<(f32, DocAddress)> = offset: opts.offset,\n218\t            sort: opts.sort,\n219\t        };\n220\t        let fallback_query = build_query(&searcher, &fields, &fuzzy_opts, &schema)?;\n221\t        top_docs = searcher\n222\t            .search(&fallback_query, &TopDocs::with_limit(fetch_limit))\n223\t            .map_err(|e| {\n224\t                AgentScribeError::DataDir(format!(\"Fuzzy fallback search let top_docs: Vec<_> = top_docs.into_iter().skip(opts.offset).collect();\n233\t\n234\t    // When token_budget is set, convert all fetched candidates so the knapsack\n235\t    // can select optimally; break;\n247\t        }\n248\t        if let Some(result) = doc_to_search_result(&searcher, &fields, *doc_addr, *score, opts) {\n249\t            results.push(result);\n250\t        }\n251\t    results = knapsack_pack(results, budget);\n259\t    }\n260\t\n261\t    let elapsed = start.elapsed();\n262\t    let query_display = opts\n263\t        .query\n264\t        .clone()\n265\t        .or_else(|| opts.error_pattern.clone())\n266\t        .or_else(|| opts.code_query.clone())\n267\t        .or_else(|| opts.like_session.clone())\n268\t        .or_else(|| Build a Tantivy query from search options.\n282\tfn build_query(\n283\t    searcher: &Searcher,\n284\t    fields: &IndexFields,\n285\t    opts: &SearchOptions,\n286\t    _schema: &tantivy::schema::Schema,\n287\t) -> Result<Box<dyn Query>> {\n288\t    // Validate that at least one search mode or filter is specified\n289\t    if opts.query.is_none()\n290\t        && opts.error_pattern.is_none()\n291\t        && opts.code_query.is_none()\n292\t        && opts.like_session.is_none()\n293\t        && opts.file_path.is_none()\n294\t        && opts.fuzzy_distance)?\n308\t        } else {\n309\t            build_fulltext_query(searcher, fields, query_str)?\n310\t        };\n311\t        clauses.push((Occur::Must, query));\n312\t error_pat);\n317\t        let query = Box::new(FuzzyTermQuery::new(term, 1, true));\n318\t        clauses.push((Occur::Must, query));\n319\t    }\n320\t\n321\t    // Code search\n322\t    if let Some(ref code_q) = opts.code_query {\n323\t        let text_query = if opts.fuzzy {\n324\t            build_fuzzy_query_for_field(fields.code_content, code_q, opts.fuzzy_distance)?\n325\t        } else {\n326\t            let (parsed, _errors) =\n327\t                tantivy::query::QueryParser::for_index(searcher.index(), vec![fields.code_content])\n328\t                    .parse_query_lenient(code_q);\n329\t            Box::new(parsed)\n330\t        };\n331\t        clauses.push((Occur::Must, text_query));\n332\t\n333\t        // Filter to code_artifact doc_type if searching code\n334\t        let doc_term = tantivy::schema::Term::from_field_text(fields.doc_type, \"code_artifact\");\n335\t        clauses.push((\n336\t            Occur::Must,\n337\t            Box::new(TermQuery::new(\n338\t                doc_term,\n339\t                tantivy::schema::IndexRecordOption::Basic,\n340\t            )),\n341\t        ));\n342\t\n343\t        // Language filter\n344\t        if let Some(ref lang) = opts.code_lang {\n345\t            let lang_term = tantivy::schema::Term::from_field_text(fields.code_language, lang);\n346\t            clauses.push((\n347\t                Occur::Must,\n348\t                Box::new(TermQuery::new(\n349\t                    lang_term,\n350\t                    tantivy::schema::IndexRecordOption::Basic,\n351\t                )),\n352\t            ));\n353\t        }\n354\t    } else if opts.doc_type_filter.is_none() {\n355\t        // Default: only return session documents (never mix with code_artifacts)\n356\t        let doc_term = tantivy::schema::Term::from_field_text(fields.doc_type, \"session\");\n357\t        clauses.push((\n358\t            Occur::Must,\n359\t            Box::new(TermQuery::new(\n360\t                doc_term,\n361\t                tantivy::schema::IndexRecordOption::Basic,\n362\t            )),\n363\t        ));\n364\t !query_str.is_empty() {\n370\t            let (parsed, _errors) = tantivy::query::QueryParser::for_index(\n371\t                searcher.index(),\n372\t                vec![fields.solution_summary],\n373\t            )\n374\t            .parse_query_lenient(query_str);\n375\t            clauses.push((Occur::Should, Box::new(parsed)));\n376\t        }\n377\t build_more_like_this(searcher, fields, like_id)?;\n382\t        clauses.push((Occur::Must, mlt_query));\n383\t        // Exclude the source session from results\n384\t        let exclude_term = Occur::Must,\n400\t            Box::new(TermQuery::new(\n401\t                term,\n402\t                tantivy::schema::IndexRecordOption::Basic,\n403\t            )),\n404\t        ));\n405\t clauses.push((\n411\t            Occur::Must,\n412\t            Box::new(TermQuery::new(\n413\t                term,\n414\t                tantivy::schema::IndexRecordOption::Basic,\n415\t Bound::Included(tantivy_since),\n425\t            Bound::Unbounded,\n426\t        );\n427\t        clauses.push((Occur::Must, Box::new(range)));\n428\t    }\n429\t\n430\t    if let Some(before) = \"timestamp\".to_string(),\n434\t            Bound::Unbounded,\n435\t            Bound::Excluded(tantivy_before),\n436\t        );\n437\t        clauses.push((Occur::Must, Box::new(range)));\n438\t  clauses.push((\n444\t            Occur::Must,\n445\t            Box::new(TermQuery::new(\n446\t                term,\n447\t                tantivy::schema::IndexRecordOption::Basic,\n448\t outcome);\n455\t        clauses.push((\n456\t            Occur::Must,\n457\t            Box::new(TermQuery::new(\n458\t                term,\n459\t                tantivy::schema::IndexRecordOption::Basic,\n460\t            )),\n461\t        ));\n462\t    }\n463\t\n464\t    // Doc type filter\n465\t    if let Some(ref dt) = opts.doc_type_filter {\n466\t term,\n471\t                tantivy::schema::IndexRecordOption::Basic,\n472\t            )),\n473\t        ));\n474\t    }\n475\t\n476\t    // Model filter\n477\t    if let Some(ref model) = opts.model {\n478\t        let term = tantivy::schema::Term::from_field_text(fields.model, model);\n479\t        clauses.push((\n480\t            Occur::Must,\n481\t            Box::new(TermQuery::new(\n482\t                term,\n483\t                tantivy::schema::IndexRecordOption::Basic,\n484\t            )),\n485\t        ));\n486\t    }\n487\t\n488\t    // File path    )),\n497\t        ));\n498\t    }\n499\t\n500\t    // Git commit filter (exact term match on stored git_commits data_dir.join(\"index\").join(INDEX_DIR_NAME);\n\n    if !index_path.exists() {\n        return Err(AgentScribeError::DataDir(format!(\n            \"Index not found at {}. retry with fuzzy.\n    // Only triggers for plain queries (not error/code/like searches) and when --fuzzy was\n    // not already set (to avoid double-fuzzing an explicit fuzzy request).\n    let mut used_fuzzy_fallback = false;\n    if top_docs.is_empty()\n        && opts.query.is_some()\n        && !opts.fuzzy\n        && opts.error_pattern.is_none()\n        && opts.code_query.is_none()\n        && opts.like_session.is_none()\n    {\n        let fuzzy_opts = SearchOptions {\n            fuzzy: true,\n            fuzzy_distance: opts.fuzzy_distance,\n            query: opts.query.clone(),\n            error_pattern: opts.error_pattern.clone(),\n            code_query: opts.code_query.clone(),\n            code_lang: opts.code_lang.clone(),\n            solution_only: opts.solution_only,\n since: opts.since,\n            before: opts.before,\n            tag: opts.tag.clone(),\n            outcome: opts.outcome.clone(),\n            doc_type_filter: opts.doc_type_filter.clone(),\n            model: opts.model.clone(),\n            file_path: opts.file_path.clone(),\n            git_commit: opts.git_commit.clone(),\n            max_results: opts.max_results,\n            snippet_length: opts.snippet_length,\n            token_budget: opts.token_budget,\n            offset: opts.offset,\n            sort: opts.sort,\n        };\n        let fallback_query = build_query(&searcher, &fields, &fuzzy_opts, &schema)?;\n        top_docs = searcher\n            .search(&fallback_query, &TopDocs::with_limit(fetch_limit))\n            .map_err(|e| {\n                AgentScribeError::DataDir(format!(\"Fuzzy fallback search failed: {}\", e))\n            })?;\n        if !top_docs.is_empty() {\n            used_fuzzy_fallback = true;\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Apply offset\n    let top_docs: Vec<_> = top_docs.into_iter().skip(opts.offset).collect();\n\n    // When token_budget is set, convert all fetched candidates so the knapsack\n    // can select optimally; otherwise cap at max_results.\n    let result_limit = if opts.token_budget.is_some() {\n        fetch_limit\n    } else {\n        opts.max_results\n    };\n\n    // Convert to SearchResult\n    let mut results: Vec<SearchResult> = Vec::new();\n    for (score, doc_addr) in &top_docs {\n        if results.len() >= result_limit {\n            break;\n        }\n        if let Some(result) = doc_to_search_result(&searcher, &fields, *doc_addr, *score, opts) {\n            results.push(result);\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Apply sort order\n    apply_sort(&mut results, opts.sort);\n\n    // Apply token budget if specified\n    if let Some(budget) = opts.token_budget {\n        results = knapsack_pack(results, budget);\n    }\n\n    let elapsed = start.elapsed();\n    let query_display = opts\n        .query\n        .clone()\n        .or_else(|| opts.error_pattern.clone())\n        .or_else(|| opts.code_query.clone())\n        .or_else(|| opts.like_session.clone())\n        .or_else(|| opts.session_id.clone())\n        .unwrap_or_default();\n\n    Ok(SearchOutput {\n        query: query_display,\n        total_matches: results.len(),\n        search_time_ms: elapsed.as_millis() as u64,\n        sessions_searched: total_docs,\n        results,\n        fuzzy_fallback: used_fuzzy_fallback,\n    })\n}\n\n/// Build a Tantivy query from search options.\nfn build_query(\n    searcher: &Searcher,\n    fields: &IndexFields,\n    opts: &SearchOptions,\n    _schema: &tantivy::schema::Schema,\n) -> Result<Box<dyn Query>> {\n    // Validate that at least one search mode or filter is specified\n    if opts.query.is_none()\n        && opts.error_pattern.is_none()\n        && opts.code_query.is_none()\n        && opts.like_session.is_none()\n        && opts.file_path.is_none()\n        && opts.git_commit.is_none()\n    {\n        return Err(AgentScribeError::DataDir(\n            \"No search query provided. Use <query>, --error, --code, --like, or a filter.\"\n                .to_string(),\n        ));\n    }\n\n    let mut clauses: Vec<(Occur, Box<dyn Query>)> = Vec::new();\n\n    // Main text query\n    if let Some(ref query_str) = opts.query {\n        let query = if opts.fuzzy {\n            build_fuzzy_query(fields, query_str, opts.fuzzy_distance)?\n        } else {\n            build_fulltext_query(searcher, fields, query_str)?\n        };\n        clauses.push((Occur::Must, query));\n    }\n\n    // Error fingerprint lookup\n    if let Some(ref error_pat) = opts.error_pattern {\n        let term = tantivy::schema::Term::from_field_text(fields.error_fingerprint, error_pat);\n        let query = Box::new(FuzzyTermQuery::new(term, 1, true));\n        clauses.push((Occur::Must, query));\n    }\n\n    // Code search\n    if let Some(ref code_q) = opts.code_query {\n        let text_query = if opts.fuzzy {\n            build_fuzzy_query_for_field(fields.code_content, code_q, opts.fuzzy_distance)?\n        } else {\n            let (parsed, _errors) =\n                tantivy::query::QueryParser::for_index(searcher.index(), vec![fields.code_content])\n                    .parse_query_lenient(code_q);\n            Box::new(parsed)\n        };\n        clauses.push((Occur::Must, text_query));\n\n        // Filter to code_artifact doc_type if searching code\n        let doc_term = tantivy::schema::Term::from_field_text(fields.doc_type, \"code_artifact\");\n        clauses.push((\n            Occur::Must,\n            Box::new(TermQuery::new(\n                doc_term,\n                tantivy::schema::IndexRecordOption::Basic,\n            )),\n        ));\n\n        // Language filter\n        if let Some(ref lang) = opts.code_lang {\n            let lang_term = tantivy::schema::Term::from_field_text(fields.code_language, lang);\n            clauses.push((\n                Occur::Must,\n                Box::new(TermQuery::new(\n                    lang_term,\n                    tantivy::schema::IndexRecordOption::Basic,\n                )),\n            ));\n        }\n    } else if opts.doc_type_filter.is_none() {\n        // Default: only return session documents (never mix with code_artifacts)\n        let doc_term = tantivy::schema::Term::from_field_text(fields.doc_type, \"session\");\n        clauses.push((\n            Occur::Must,\n            Box::new(TermQuery::new(\n                doc_term,\n                tantivy::schema::IndexRecordOption::Basic,\n            )),\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Solution-only: boost solution_summary field\n    if opts.solution_only {\n        let query_str = opts.query.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"\");\n        if !query_str.is_empty() {\n            let (parsed, _errors) = tantivy::query::QueryParser::for_index(\n                searcher.index(),\n                vec![fields.solution_summary],\n            )\n            .parse_query_lenient(query_str);\n            clauses.push((Occur::Should, Box::new(parsed)));\n        }\n    }\n\n    // --like <session-id>: find sessions with similar content (TF-IDF based MLT)\n    if let Some(ref like_id) = opts.like_session {\n        let mlt_query = build_more_like_this(searcher, fields, like_id)?;\n        clauses.push((Occur::Must, mlt_query));\n        // Exclude the source session from results\n        let exclude_term = tantivy::schema::Term::from_field_text(fields.session_id, like_id);\n        clauses.push((\n            Occur::MustNot,\n            Box::new(TermQuery::new(\n                exclude_term,\n                tantivy::schema::IndexRecordOption::Basic,\n            )),\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Filters (all as Must clauses)\n    // Agent filter\n    for agent_name in &opts.agent {\n        let term = tantivy::schema::Term::from_field_text(fields.source_agent, agent_name);\n        clauses.push((\n            Occur::Must,\n            Box::new(TermQuery::new(\n                term,\n                tantivy::schema::IndexRecordOption::Basic,\n            )),\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Project filter\n    if let Some(ref project) = opts.project {\n        let term = tantivy::schema::Term::from_field_text(fields.project, project);\n        clauses.push((\n            Occur::Must,\n            Box::new(TermQuery::new(\n                term,\n                tantivy::schema::IndexRecordOption::Basic,\n            )),\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Date range filters\n    if let Some(since) = opts.since {\n        let tantivy_since = TantivyDateTime::from_timestamp_secs(since.timestamp());\n        let range = RangeQuery::new_date_bounds(\n            \"timestamp\".to_string(),\n            Bound::Included(tantivy_since),\n            Bound::Unbounded,\n        );\n        clauses.push((Occur::Must, Box::new(range)));\n    }\n\n    if let Some(before) = opts.before {\n        let tantivy_before = TantivyDateTime::from_timestamp_secs(before.timestamp());\n        let range = RangeQuery::new_date_bounds(\n            \"timestamp\".to_string(),\n            Bound::Unbounded,\n            Bound::Excluded(tantivy_before),\n        );\n        clauses.push((Occur::Must, Box::new(range)));\n    }\n\n    // Tag filter (AND logic - all tags must be present)\n    for tag in &opts.tag {\n        let term = tantivy::schema::Term::from_field_text(fields.tags, tag);\n        clauses.push((\n            Occur::Must,\n            Box::new(TermQuery::new(\n                term,\n                tantivy::schema::IndexRecordOption::Basic,\n            )),\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Outcome filter\n    if let Some(ref outcome) = opts.outcome {\n        let term = tantivy::schema::Term::from_field_text(fields.outcome, outcome);\n        clauses.push((\n            Occur::Must,\n            Box::new(TermQuery::new(\n                term,\n                tantivy::schema::IndexRecordOption::Basic,\n            )),\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Doc type filter\n    if let Some(ref dt) = opts.doc_type_filter {\n        let term = tantivy::schema::Term::from_field_text(fields.doc_type, dt);\n        clauses.push((\n            Occur::Must,\n            Box::new(TermQuery::new(\n                term,\n                tantivy::schema::IndexRecordOption::Basic,\n            )),\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Model filter\n    if let Some(ref model) = opts.model {\n        let term = tantivy::schema::Term::from_field_text(fields.model, model);\n        clauses.push((\n            Occur::Must,\n            Box::new(TermQuery::new(\n                term,\n                tantivy::schema::IndexRecordOption::Basic,\n            )),\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // File path filter (exact term match on stored file_paths values)\n    if let Some(ref fp) = opts.file_path {\n        let term = tantivy::schema::Term::from_field_text(fields.file_paths, fp);\n        clauses.push((\n            Occur::Must,\n            Box::new(TermQuery::new(\n                term,\n                tantivy::schema::IndexRecordOption::Basic,\n            )),\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Git commit filter (exact term match on stored git_commits values)","numLines":500,"startLine":1,"totalLines":5107}}}

\"you\",\n612\t];\n613\t\n614\t/// Check if a token is a stop word.\n615\tfn is_stop_word(word: &str) -> bool {\n616\t    MLT_STOP_WORDS.binary_search(&word).is_ok()\n617\t}\n618\t\n619\t/// Build a \"more like this\" query using TF-IDF weighted term extraction.\n620\t///\n621\t/// Extracts terms from the source document's content and summary fields,\n622\t/// ranks them by TF-IDF significance, and builds a boosted boolean query\n623\t/// from the top-scoring terms. The caller is responsible for excluding\n624\t/// the original session via a MustNot clause at the outer query level.\n625\tfn build_more_like_this(\n626\t    searcher: &Searcher,\n627\t    fields: &IndexFields,\n628\t    session_id: &str,\n629\t) -> Result<Box<dyn Query>> {\n630\t    let term = tantivy::schema::Term::from_field_text(fields.session_id, session_id);\n631\t    let query = .map_err(|e| AgentScribeError::DataDir(format!(\"Search failed: {}\", e)))?;\n636\t\n637\t    if docs.is_empty() {\n638\t        return Err(AgentScribeError::DataDir(format!(\n639\t            \"Session '{}' not found in index\",\n640\t            session_id\n641\t        )));\n642\t    }\n643\t\n644\t    let doc: TantivyDocument = searcher\n645\t        .doc(docs[0].1)\n646\t        .map_err(|e| AgentScribeError::DataDir(format!(\"Failed to fetch document: {}\", e)))?;\n647\t\n648\t    // Collect text from content, summary, and tags fields\n649\t    let mut all_text = String::new();\n650\t    for field in [fields.content, fields.summary] {\n651\t        if let Some(text) = doc.get_first(field).and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {\n652\t            all_text.push_str(text);\n653\t            all_text.push(' ');\n654\t        }\n655\t    }\n656\t    // Tags are highly discriminative — include them\n657\t    for tag_val in doc.get_all(fields.tags).filter_map(|v| v.as_str()) {\n658\t        all_text.push_str(tag_val);\n659\t        all_text.push(' ');\n660\t    }\n661\t\n662\t    // Tokenize and compute term frequencies, filtering stop words and short tokens\n663\t    let mut tf_counts: std::collections::HashMap<String, usize> = std::collections::HashMap::new();\n664\t    let total_tokens: usize = all_text\n665\t        .split_whitespace()\n666\t        .filter(|w| w.len() > 2)\n667\t        .map(|w| w.to_lowercase())\n668\t        .filter(|w| !is_stop_word(w))\n669\t        .inspect(|w| {\n670\t            *tf_counts.entry(w.clone()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n671\t        })\n672\t        .count();\n673\t\n674\t    if total_tokens == 0 {\n675\t        return Err(AgentScribeError::DataDir(\n676\t            \"No content terms found in session\".to_string(),\n677\t        ));\n678\t    }\n679\t\n680\t    // Compute TF-IDF for each unique term\n681\t    let num_docs = searcher.num_docs() as f64;\n682\t    let mut scored_terms: Vec<(String, f64)> = tf_counts\n683\t        .into_iter()\n684\t        .filter_map(|(term_str, tf)| {\n685\t            let term = tantivy::schema::Term::from_field_text(fields.content, &term_str);\n686\t            let df = searcher.doc_freq(&term).unwrap_or(1) as f64;\n687\t            if df < 1.0 {\n688\t                return None;\n689\t            }\n690\t            let idf = (num_docs / df).ln();\n691\t            let tf_norm = tf as f64 / total_tokens as f64;\n692\t            let tfidf = tf_norm * idf;\n693\t            Some((term_str, tfidf))\n694\t        })\n695\t        .collect();\n696\t\n697\t    // Sort by TF-IDF descending — most significant terms first\n698\t    scored_terms.sort_by(|a, b| b.1.partial_cmp(&a.1).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));\n699\t\n700\t    // Take top 20 significant terms\n701\t    let top_terms: Vec<_> = scored_terms.into_iter().take(20).collect();\n702\t\n703\t    #[cfg(test)]\n704\t    eprintln!(\n705\t        \"DEBUG MLT: num_docs={}, total_tokens={}, top_terms={:?}\",\n706\t        num_docs,\n707\t        total_tokens,\n708\t        top_terms\n709\t            .iter()\n710\t            .map(|(t, s)| format!(\"{}={:.4}\", t, s))\n711\t            .collect::<Vec<_>>()\n712\t    );\n713\t\n714\t    if top_terms.is_empty() {\n715\t        return Err(AgentScribeError::DataDir(\n716\t            \"No significant terms found in session\".to_string(),\n717\t        ));\n718\t    }\n719\t\n720\t    // Build MLT query: each term as a Should clause, boosted by relative TF-IDF\n721\t    let max_score = top_terms[0].1;\n722\t    let mlt_clauses: Vec<(Occur, Box<dyn Query>)> = Box::new(TermQuery::new(\n731\t                        term,\n732\t                        tantivy::schema::IndexRecordOption::Basic,\n733\t                    )),\n734\t                    boost,\n735\t                )) as Box<dyn Query>,\n736\t            )\n737\t        })\n738\t        .collect();\n739\t\n740\t    Ok(Box::new(BooleanQuery::new(mlt_clauses)))\n741\t}\n742\t\n743\t/// Convert a Tantivy document to a SearchResult.\n744\tfn doc_to_search_result(\n745\t    searcher: &Searcher,\n746\t    fields: &IndexFields,\n747\t    doc_addr: DocAddress,\n748\t    score: f32,\n749\t    opts: &SearchOptions,\n750\t) -> Option<SearchResult> {\n751\t    let doc: TantivyDocument = searcher.doc(doc_addr).ok()?;\n752\t\n753\t    let session_id = doc\n754\t        .get_first(fields.session_id)\n755\t        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n756\t        .unwrap_or(\"\")\n757\t        .to_string();\n758\t\n759\t    let source_agent = doc\n760\t        .get_first(fields.source_agent)\n761\t        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n762\t        .unwrap_or(\"\")\n763\t        .to_string();\n764\t\n765\t    let project = doc\n766\t        .get_first(fields.project)\n767\t        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n768\t        .map(|s| s.to_string());\n769\t\n770\t    let timestamp = doc\n771\t        .get_first(fields.timestamp)\n772\t .get_first(fields.outcome)\n783\t        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n784\t        .map(|s| s.to_string());\n785\t\n786\t    let summary = doc\n787\t        .get_first(fields.summary)\n788\t        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n789\t        .map(|s| s.to_string());\n790\t\n791\t    let doc_type = doc\n792\t        .get_first(fields.doc_type)\n793\t        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n794\t        .map(|s| s.to_string());\n795\t\n796\t    let model = doc\n797\t        .get_first(fields.model)\n798\t        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n799\t        .map(|s| s.to_string());\n800\t\n801\t    //      None\n819\t    };\n820\t\n821\t    // Collect tags\n822\t    let tags: Vec<String> = doc\n823\t        .get_all(fields.tags)\n824\t        .filter_map(|v| v.as_str())\n825\t        .map(|s| s.to_string())\n826\t        .collect();\n827\t\n828\t    // Estimate token cost: ceil((snippet_chars + summary_chars) / CHARS_PER_TOKEN)\n829\t    let token_count = {\n830\t        let snippet_chars summary_chars).div_ceil(CHARS_PER_TOKEN)\n833\t    };\n834\t\n835\t    Some(SearchResult {\n836\t        session_id,\n837\t        source_agent,\n838\t        project,\n839\t        timestamp,\n840\t token_count,\n849\t    })\n850\t}\n851\t\n852\t/// Extract a snippet from content around the best match.\n853\tfn extract_snippet(content: &str, max_length: usize) -> Option<String> {\n854\t    if content.is_empty() || max_length == 0 {\n855\t        return None;\n856\t    }\n857\t\n858\t    if content.len() <= max_length {\n859\t        return Some(content.to_string());\n860\t    }\n861\t\n862\t    // Take from the beginning up to max_length, at a word boundary\n863\t    let mut end = max_length;\n864\t    while end > 0 && end < content.len() && !content.is_char_boundary(end) {\n865\t        end += 1;\n866\t    }\n867\t\n868\t    // Try to break at a word boundary\n869\t    if let Some(space_pos) = content[..end].rfind(' ') {\n870\t        end = space_pos;\n871\t    }\n872\t\n873\t    let snippet = &content[..end];\n874\t    if snippet.is_empty() {\n875\t        None\n876\t    } else {\n877\t        Some(format!(\"{}...\", snippet.trim_end()))\n878\t    }\n879\t}\n880\t\n881\t/// Look up a specific session by ID.\n882\tfn lookup_session(\n883\t    searcher: &Searcher,\n884\t    session_id: &str,\n885\t    start: &std::time::Instant,\n886\t    total_docs: u64,\n887\t) -> Result<SearchOutput> {\n888\t    let (_schema, fields) = build_schema();\n889\t\n890\t    let term = tantivy::schema::Term::from_field_text(fields.session_id, &IndexFields,\n    query_str: &str,\n    distance: u8,\n) -> Result<Box<dyn Query>> {\n    let terms: Vec<(Occur, Box<dyn Query>)> = query_str\n        .split_whitespace()\n        .filter(|w| !w.is_empty())\n        .flat_map(|word| {\n            let mut sub = Vec::new();\n            let term_content = tantivy::schema::Term::from_field_text(fields.content, word);\n Occur::Should,\n                Box::new(FuzzyTermQuery::new(term_solution, distance, true)) as Box<dyn Query>,\n            ));\n            sub\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    if terms.is_empty() {\n        return Err(AgentScribeError::DataDir(\"Empty query string\".to_string()));\n    }\n\n    Ok(Box::new(BooleanQuery::new(terms)))\n}\n\n/// Build a fuzzy query for a specific field.\nfn build_fuzzy_query_for_field(\n    field: Field,\n    query_str: &str,\n    distance: u8,\n) -> Result<Box<dyn Query>> {\n    let terms: Vec<(Occur, Box<dyn Query>)> = query_str\n        .split_whitespace()\n        .filter(|w| !w.is_empty())\n        .map(|word| {\n            let term = tantivy::schema::Term::from_field_text(field, word);\n            (\n Must be sorted alphabetically for binary_search.\nstatic MLT_STOP_WORDS: &[&str] = &[\n    \"about\", \"after\", \"all\", \"also\", \"and\", \"are\", \"because\", \"been\", \"being\", \"but\", \"can\",\n    \"could\", \"did\", \"does\", \"doing\", \"each\", \"for\", \"from\", \"get\", \"got\", \"had\", \"has\", \"have\",\n    \"her\", \"how\", \"into\", \"its\", \"just\", \"like\", \"make\", \"may\", \"new\", \"not\", \"now\", \"old\", \"one\",\n    \"other\", \"our\", \"out\", \"over\", \"see\", \"should\", \"some\", \"such\", \"than\", \"that\", \"the\", \"their\",\n    \"them\", \"then\", \"there\", \"these\", \"this\", \"was\", \"way\", \"were\", \"who\", \"will\", \"with\", \"would\",\n    \"you\",\n];\n\n/// Check if a token is a stop word.\nfn is_stop_word(word: &str) -> bool {\n    MLT_STOP_WORDS.binary_search(&word).is_ok()\n}\n\n/// Build a \"more like this\" query using TF-IDF weighted term extraction.\n///\n/// Extracts terms from the source document's content and summary fields,\n/// ranks them by TF-IDF significance, and builds a boosted boolean query\n/// from the top-scoring terms. The caller is responsible for excluding\n/// the original session via a MustNot clause at the outer query level.\nfn build_more_like_this(\n    searcher: &Searcher,\n    fields: &IndexFields,\n    session_id: &str,\n) -> Result<Box<dyn Query>> {\n    let term = tantivy::schema::Term::from_field_text(fields.session_id, session_id);\n    let query = TermQuery::new(term, tantivy::schema::IndexRecordOption::Basic);\n\n    let docs: Vec<(f32, DocAddress)> = searcher\n        .search(&query, &TopDocs::with_limit(1))\n        .map_err(|e| AgentScribeError::DataDir(format!(\"Search failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n    if docs.is_empty() {\n        return Err(AgentScribeError::DataDir(format!(\n            \"Session '{}' not found in index\",\n            session_id\n        )));\n    }\n\n    let doc: TantivyDocument = searcher\n        .doc(docs[0].1)\n        .map_err(|e| AgentScribeError::DataDir(format!(\"Failed to fetch document: {}\", e)))?;\n\n    // Collect text from content, summary, and tags fields\n    let mut all_text = String::new();\n    for field in [fields.content, fields.summary] {\n        if let Some(text) = doc.get_first(field).and_then(|v| v.as_str()) {\n            all_text.push_str(text);\n            all_text.push(' ');\n        }\n    }\n    // Tags are highly discriminative — include them\n    for tag_val in doc.get_all(fields.tags).filter_map(|v| v.as_str()) {\n        all_text.push_str(tag_val);\n        all_text.push(' ');\n    }\n\n    // Tokenize and compute term frequencies, filtering stop words and short tokens\n    let mut tf_counts: std::collections::HashMap<String, usize> = std::collections::HashMap::new();\n    let total_tokens: usize = all_text\n        .split_whitespace()\n        .filter(|w| w.len() > 2)\n        .map(|w| w.to_lowercase())\n        .filter(|w| !is_stop_word(w))\n        .inspect(|w| {\n            *tf_counts.entry(w.clone()).or_insert(0) += 1;\n        })\n        .count();\n\n    if total_tokens == 0 {\n        return Err(AgentScribeError::DataDir(\n            \"No content terms found in session\".to_string(),\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Compute TF-IDF for each unique term\n    let num_docs = searcher.num_docs() as f64;\n    let mut scored_terms: Vec<(String, f64)> = tf_counts\n        .into_iter()\n        .filter_map(|(term_str, tf)| {\n            let term = tantivy::schema::Term::from_field_text(fields.content, &term_str);\n            let df = searcher.doc_freq(&term).unwrap_or(1) as f64;\n            if df < 1.0 {\n                return None;\n            }\n            let idf = (num_docs / df).ln();\n            let tf_norm = tf as f64 / total_tokens as f64;\n            let tfidf = tf_norm * idf;\n            Some((term_str, tfidf))\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    // Sort by TF-IDF descending — most significant terms first\n    scored_terms.sort_by(|a, b| b.1.partial_cmp(&a.1).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal));\n\n    // Take top 20 significant terms\n    let top_terms: Vec<_> = scored_terms.into_iter().take(20).collect();\n\n    #[cfg(test)]\n    eprintln!(\n        \"DEBUG MLT: num_docs={}, total_tokens={}, top_terms={:?}\",\n        num_docs,\n        total_tokens,\n        top_terms\n            .iter()\n            .map(|(t, s)| session\".to_string(),\n        ));\n    }\n\n    // Build MLT query: each term as a Should clause, boosted by relative TF-IDF\n    let max_score = top_terms[0].1;\n    let mlt_clauses: Vec<(Occur, Box<dyn Query>)> = top_terms\n        .iter()\n        .map(|(term_str, score)| {\n            let term = tantivy::schema::Term::from_field_text(fields.content, term_str);\n            let boost = (score / max_score * 2.0 + 0.5) as f32;\n            (\n                Occur::Should,\n                Box::new(BoostQuery::new(\n                    Box::new(TermQuery::new(\n                        term,\n                        tantivy::schema::IndexRecordOption::Basic,\n                    )),\n                    boost,\n                )) as Box<dyn Query>,\n            )\n        })\n        .collect();\n\n    Ok(Box::new(BooleanQuery::new(mlt_clauses)))\n}\n\n/// Convert a Tantivy document to a SearchResult.\nfn doc_to_search_result(\n    searcher: &Searcher,\n    fields: &IndexFields,\n    doc_addr: DocAddress,\n    score: f32,\n    opts: &SearchOptions,\n) -> Option<SearchResult> {\n    let doc: TantivyDocument = searcher.doc(doc_addr).ok()?;\n\n    let session_id = s.to_string());\n\n    let timestamp = doc\n        .get_first(fields.timestamp)\n        .and_then(|v| v.as_datetime())\n        .map(|dt: tantivy::DateTime| {\n            DateTime::from_timestamp(dt.into_timestamp_secs(), 0)\n                .unwrap_or_default()\n                .to_rfc3339()\n        });\n\n    let turns = doc.get_first(fields.turn_count).and_then(|v| v.as_u64());\n\n    let outcome = doc\n        .get_first(fields.outcome)\n        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n        .map(|s| s.to_string());\n\n    let summary = doc\n        .get_first(fields.summary)\n        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n        .map(|s| s.to_string());\n\n    let doc_type = doc\n        .get_first(fields.doc_type)\n        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n        .map(|s| s.to_string());\n\n    let model = doc\n        .get_first(fields.model)\n        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n        .map(|s| s.to_string());\n\n    // Extract snippet\n    let snippet = if opts.snippet_length > 0 {\n        let content_field = if opts.solution_only {\n            fields.solution_summary\n        } else if opts.code_query.is_some() {\n            fields.code_content\n v.as_str())\n        .map(|s| s.to_string())\n        .collect();\n\n    // Estimate token cost: ceil((snippet_chars + summary_chars) / CHARS_PER_TOKEN)\n    let token_count = {\n        let summary_chars).div_ceil(CHARS_PER_TOKEN)\n    };\n\n    Some(SearchResult {\n        session_id,\n        source_agent,\n        project,\n        timestamp,\n        turns,\n        outcome,\n        score,\n        summary,\n        snippet,\n        tags,\n        doc_type,\n        model,\n        token_count,\n    })\n}\n\n/// Extract a snippet from content around the best match.\nfn extract_snippet(content: &str, max_length: usize) -> Option<String> {\n    if content.is_empty() || max_length == 0 {\n        return None;\n    }\n\n    if content.len() <= max_length {\n        return Some(content.to_string());\n    }\n\n    // Take from the beginning up to max_length, at a word boundary\n    let mut end = max_length;\n    while end > 0 && end < content.len() && !content.is_char_boundary(end) {\n        end += 1;\n    }\n\n    // Try to break at a word boundary\n    if let Some(space_pos) = content[..end].rfind(' ') {\n        end = space_pos;\n    }\n\n    let snippet = &content[..end];\n    if snippet.is_empty() {\n        None\n    } else {\n        Some(format!(\"{}...\", snippet.trim_end()))\n    }\n}\n\n/// Look up a specific session by ID.\nfn lookup_session(\n    searcher: &Searcher,\n    session_id: &str,\n    start: &std::time::Instant,\n    total_docs: u64,\n) -> Result<SearchOutput> {\n    let (_schema, fields) = build_schema();\n\n DocAddress)> = searcher\n        .search(&query, &TopDocs::with_limit(1))\n        .map_err(|e| AgentScribeError::DataDir(format!(\"Search failed: {}\", e)))?;\n\n    let mut results = Vec::new();\n match sort {\n        SortOrder::Relevance => {\n            // Already sorted by BM25 score (descending)\n            results.sort_by(|a, b| {\n                b.score\n                    .partial_cmp(&a.score)\n                    .unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal)\n            });\n        }\n        SortOrder::Newest => {\n            results.sort_by(|a, b| {\n                let SortOrder::Oldest => {\n            results.sort_by(|a, b| {\n                let ts_a = a.timestamp.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"\");\n                let ts_b = b.timestamp.as_deref().unwrap_or(\"\");\n                ts_a.cmp(ts_b)\n            });\n        }\n        SortOrder::Turns => {\n            results.sort_by_key(|b| std::cmp::Reverse(b.turns.unwrap_or(0)));\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n/// Greedy greedily selected. When a result's full\n/// snippet doesn't fit in the remaining budget, the snippet is truncated to fill\n/// the remaining space rather than skipping the result entirely — this is the\n/// adaptive behaviour that trades fewer/longer snippets for more/shorter ones.\nfn knapsack_pack(results: Vec<SearchResult>, token_budget: usize) -> Vec<SearchResult> {\n    // Sort by score descending (greedy by relevance)\n    let mut items = results;\n    items.sort_by(|a, b| {\n        b.score\n            .partial_cmp(&a.score)\n            .unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal)\n    });\n\n    let mut selected = Vec::new();\n    let mut remaining = token_budget;\n\n    for mut result in items {\n        if remaining == 0 {\n            break;\n        }\n\n        let snippet_chars = result.snippet.as_ref().map(|s| s.len()).unwrap_or(0);\n        let summary_chars = result.summary.as_ref().map(|s| s.len()).unwrap_or(0);\n        let full_cost = (snippet_chars + summary_chars).div_ceil(CHARS_PER_TOKEN);","numLines":500,"startLine":500,"totalLines":5107}}}

usize) -> Self {\n1211\t        let solution_tokens = Self::count_tokens(&self.past_solutions);\n1212\t        let conventions_tokens = Self::count_tokens(&self.conventions);\n1213\t        let               remaining = 0;\n1234\t            } else {\n1235\t                remaining -= conventions_tokens;\n1236\t            }\n1237\t        } else {\n1238\t            self.conventions =        }\n1247\t        } else {\n1248\t            self.file_notes = String::new();\n1249\t        }\n1250\t\n1251\t        self\n1252\t    }\n1253\t}\n1254\t\n1255\t/// Truncate text to a maximum character count at a word boundary.\n1256\tfn truncate_to_chars(text: &str, max_chars: usize) -> String {\n1257\t    if text.len() <= max_chars {\n1258\t        return text.to_string();\n1259\t    }\n1260\t\n1261\t    // Find the last word boundary before max_chars\n1262\t    let mut end = max_chars;\n1263\t    while end > 0 && !text.is_char_boundary(end) {\n1264\t        end += 1;\n1265\t word in text.split_whitespace() {\n        if line_len == 0 {\n            result.push_str(word);\n            line_len = word.len();\n        } else if line_len + word.len() < width {\n result.snippet {\n                for line in snippet.lines().take(3) {\n                    lines.push(format!(\"  {}\", line));\n                }\n            }\n        }\n        lines.join(\"\\n\")\n    };\n\n    // Step 2: Project conventions (inline rules extraction)\n    let conventions = if let Some(proj) = project_path {\n        crate::rules::extract_rules_inline(data_dir, proj)?\n    } else {\n        String::new()\n    };\n\n    // Step 3: File notes (extract file paths from task and get knowledge)\n    let file_paths = extract_file_paths(task);\n    let mut file_notes = Vec::new();\n    for path in &file_paths {\n        if let Ok(knowledge) = crate::file_knowledge::build_file_knowledge(\n            data_dir,\n            path,\n            &crate::config::Config::default(),\n        ) {\n            if !knowledge.gotchas.is_empty() || !knowledge.error_patterns.is_empty() {\n                file_notes.push(format!(\"**{}**\", path));\n                for gotcha in &knowledge.gotchas {\n                    file_notes.push(format!(\"  - {}\", gotcha.pattern));\n                }\n                if !knowledge.error_patterns.is_empty() {\n                    let errors: Vec<_> = knowledge\n                        .error_patterns\n                        .iter()\n                        .take(3)\n                        .map(|e| format!(\"  - Error: {}\", truncate_fingerprint(&e.fingerprint, 80)))\n                        .collect();\n                    file_notes.extend(errors);\n parse_datetime(\"2026-03-14T10:30:00Z\").unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(dt.to_rfc3339(), \"2026-03-14T10:30:00+00:00\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_datetime_date_only() {\n        let dt = parse_datetime(\"2026-03-14\").unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(dt.to_rfc3339(), \"2026-03-14T00:00:00+00:00\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_datetime_relative_hours() {\n        let dt = parse_datetime(\"24h\").unwrap();\n        let now = Utc::now();\n        let diff = now.signed_duration_since(dt);\n        assert!(diff.num_hours() >= 23 && diff.num_hours() <= 25);\n    }\n\n assert!(diff.num_days() >= 6 && diff.num_days() <= 8);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_datetime_relative_weeks() {\n        let dt = parse_datetime(\"1w\").unwrap();\n        let now = Utc::now();\n        let diff = now.signed_duration_since(dt);\n        assert!(diff.num_weeks() >= 0 && diff.num_weeks() <= 2);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_datetime_invalid() {\n        assert!(parse_datetime(\"not-a-date\").is_err());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_extract_snippet_short() {\n        let content = \"short text\";\n        assert_eq!(\n let snippet = extract_snippet(&content, 100).unwrap();\n        assert!(snippet.len() <= 110); // Allow some margin for \"...\"\n        assert!(snippet.ends_with(\"...\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n
