like_session: Some(\"claude/src\".to_string()),\n            agent: vec![\"aider\".to_string()],\n            ..default_opts()\n        };\n        let result = execute_search(temp_dir.path(), &opts).unwrap();\n        for r in &result.results {\n            assert_eq!(r.source_agent, \"aider\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_more_like_this_cross_agent() {\n        use crate::event::{Event, Role, SessionManifest};\n        use crate::index::build_session_document;\n        use tempfile::TempDir;\n\n        let temp_dir = TempDir::new().unwrap();\n        let index_path = temp_dir.path().join(\"index\").join(\"tantivy\");\n\n        let (schema, fields) = build_schema();\n        std::fs::create_dir_all(&index_path).unwrap();\n        let index = tantivy::Index::create_in_dir(&index_path, schema).unwrap();\n        let mut writer = index.writer(50_000_000).unwrap();\n\n        let now = Utc::now();\n\n        // Source: claude-code session about kubernetes deployment\n        let m1 = {\n            let mut m = SessionManifest::new(\"claude/k8s-1\".to_string(), \"claude-code\".to_string());\n            m.project = Some(\"/home/user/cluster\".to_string());\n            m.started = now;\n            m.turns = 4;\n            m.tags = vec![\"kubernetes\".to_string(), \"deployment\".to_string()];\n            m\n        };\n        let e1 = vec![\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"claude/k8s-1\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::User,\n                \"deploy the application to kubernetes cluster using kubectl\".to_string(),\n            ),\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"claude/k8s-1\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::Assistant,\n                \"created kubernetes deployment yaml manifest and applied kubectl rollout\"\n                    .to_string(),\n            ),\n        ];\n        writer\n            .add_document(build_session_document(&fields, &e1, &m1))\n            .unwrap();\n\n        // Cross-agent similar: aider session about kubernetes deployment\n        let m2 = {\n            let mut m = SessionManifest::new(\"aider/k8s-2\".to_string(), \"aider\".to_string());\n            m.project = Some(\"/home/user/other-project\".to_string());\n            m.started = now;\n            m.turns = 3;\n            m.tags = vec![\"kubernetes\".to_string(), \"helm\".to_string()];\n            m\n        };\n        let e2 = vec![\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"aider/k8s-2\".to_string(),\n                \"aider\".to_string(),\n                Role::User,\n                \"deploy kubernetes deployment for the application\".to_string(),\n            ),\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"aider/k8s-2\".to_string(),\n                \"aider\".to_string(),\n                Role::Assistant,\n                \"created kubernetes deployment yaml and applied kubectl rollout successfully\"\n                    .to_string(),\n            ),\n        ];\n        writer\n            .add_document(build_session_document(&fields, &e2, &m2))\n            .unwrap();\n\n        // Unrelated session to dilute common terms\n        let m3 = {\n            let mut m = SessionManifest::new(\n                \"claude/unrelated-css\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n            );\n            m.started = now;\n            m.turns = 2;\n            m.tags = vec![\"css\".to_string(), \"frontend\".to_string()];\n            m\n        };\n        let e3 = vec![\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"claude/unrelated-css\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::User,\n                \"fix flexbox layout\".to_string(),\n            ),\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"claude/unrelated-css\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::Assistant,\n                \"updated css grid properties\".to_string(),\n            ),\n        ];\n        writer\n            .add_document(build_session_document(&fields, &e3, &m3))\n            .unwrap();\n\n        writer.commit().unwrap();\n        writer.wait_merging_threads().unwrap();\n\n        // --like should discover the cross-agent session (no text query — MLT only)\n        let opts = SearchOptions {\n            query: None,\n            like_session: Some(\"claude/k8s-1\".to_string()),\n            ..default_opts()\n        };\n        let result = execute_search(temp_dir.path(), &opts).unwrap();\n\n        // Debug: verify terms are searchable\n        let reader = index.reader().unwrap();\n        let searcher = reader.searcher();\n        let k8s_term = tantivy::schema::Term::from_field_text(fields.content, \"kubernetes\");\n        eprintln!(\n            \"DEBUG: kubernetes doc_freq={:?}\",\n            searcher.doc_freq(&k8s_term)\n        );\n        let (_schema2, fields2) = build_schema();\n        let k8s_query = TermQuery::new(k8s_term, tantivy::schema::IndexRecordOption::Basic);\n        let k8s_docs: Vec<(f32, _)> = searcher\n            .search(&k8s_query, &TopDocs::with_limit(10))\n            .unwrap();\n        eprintln!(\"DEBUG: kubernetes query matched {} docs\", k8s_docs.len());\n        for (score, addr) in &k8s_docs {\n            let d: TantivyDocument = searcher.doc(*addr).unwrap();\n            let sid = d\n                .get_first(fields2.session_id)\n                .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                .unwrap_or(\"?\");\n            eprintln!(\"DEBUG:   {} score={}\", sid, score);\n        }\n\n        // Debug: test the MLT Should-only BooleanQuery directly\n        let mlt_query = build_more_like_this(&searcher, &fields, \"claude/k8s-1\").unwrap();\n        let mlt_docs: Vec<(f32, _)> = searcher\n            .search(&*mlt_query, &TopDocs::with_limit(10))\n            .unwrap();\n        eprintln!(\n            \"DEBUG: MLT Should-only query matched {} docs\",\n            mlt_docs.len()\n        );\n        for (score, addr) in &mlt_docs {\n            let d: TantivyDocument = searcher.doc(*addr).unwrap();\n            let sid = d\n                .get_first(fields2.session_id)\n                .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                .unwrap_or(\"?\");\n            eprintln!(\"DEBUG: MLT   {} score={}\", sid, score);\n        }\n\n        // Debug: test with MustNot at same level\n        let exclude_term =\n            tantivy::schema::Term::from_field_text(fields.session_id, \"claude/k8s-1\");\n        let combined = BooleanQuery::new(vec![\n            (Occur::Must, mlt_query),\n            (\n                Occur::MustNot,\n                Box::new(TermQuery::new(\n                    exclude_term,\n                    tantivy::schema::IndexRecordOption::Basic,\n                )) as Box<dyn Query>,\n            ),\n        ]);\n        let combined_docs: Vec<(f32, _)> = searcher\n            .search(&combined, &TopDocs::with_limit(10))\n            .unwrap();\n        eprintln!(\n            \"DEBUG: combined Must+MustNot query matched {} docs\",\n            combined_docs.len()\n        );\n        for (score, addr) in &combined_docs {\n            let d: TantivyDocument = searcher.doc(*addr).unwrap();\n            let sid = d\n                .get_first(fields2.session_id)\n                .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n                .unwrap_or(\"?\");\n            eprintln!(\"DEBUG: comb  {} score={}\", sid, score);\n        }\n\n        assert!(\n            result.total_matches >= 1,\n            \"expected at least 1 match, got {}\",\n            result.total_matches\n        );\n\n        // Should find the aider session (cross-agent discovery)\n        let cross_agent_found = result\n            .results\n            .iter()\n            .any(|r| r.session_id == \"aider/k8s-2\" && r.source_agent == \"aider\");\n        assert!(\n            cross_agent_found,\n            \"should discover cross-agent similar sessions\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    // Helper to create default search options\n    fn default_opts() -> SearchOptions {\n        SearchOptions {\n            query: Some(\"test\".to_string()),\n            error_pattern: None,\n            code_query: None,\n            code_lang: None,\n            solution_only: false,\n            like_session: None,\n            session_id: None,\n            agent: vec![],\n            project: None,\n            since: None,\n            before: None,\n            tag: vec![],\n            outcome: None,\n            doc_type_filter: None,\n            model: None,\n            file_path: None,\n            git_commit: None,\n            fuzzy: false,\n            fuzzy_distance: 1,\n            max_results: 10,\n            snippet_length: 200,\n            token_budget: None,\n            offset: 0,\n            sort: SortOrder::Relevance,\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Helper to make a test search result\n    fn make_test_result(id: &str, score: f32, text_len: usize) -> SearchResult {\n        let token_count = text_len.div_ceil(CHARS_PER_TOKEN);\n        SearchResult {\n            session_id: id.to_string(),\n            source_agent: \"test\".to_string(),\n            project: None,\n            timestamp: None,\n            turns: None,\n            outcome: None,\n            score,\n            summary: None,\n            snippet: Some(\"x\".repeat(text_len)),\n            tags: vec![],\n            doc_type: None,\n            model: None,\n            token_count,\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn make_test_result_with_ts(id: &str, score: f32, ts: &str) -> SearchResult {\n        SearchResult {\n            session_id: id.to_string(),\n            source_agent: \"test\".to_string(),\n            project: None,\n            timestamp: Some(ts.to_string()),\n            turns: None,\n            outcome: None,\n            score,\n            summary: None,\n            snippet: None,\n            tags: vec![],\n            doc_type: None,\n            model: None,\n            token_count: 0,\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn make_test_result_with_turns(id: &str, score: f32, turns: u64) -> SearchResult {\n        SearchResult {\n            session_id: id.to_string(),\n            source_agent: \"test\".to_string(),\n            project: None,\n            timestamp: None,\n            turns: Some(turns),\n            outcome: None,\n            score,\n            summary: None,\n            snippet: None,\n            tags: vec![],\n            doc_type: None,\n            model: None,\n            token_count: 0,\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_apply_sort_oldest() {\n        let mut results = vec![\n            make_test_result_with_ts(\"a\", 5.0, \"2026-03-10T00:00:00+00:00\"),\n            make_test_result_with_ts(\"b\", 10.0, \"2026-03-14T00:00:00+00:00\"),\n            make_test_result_with_ts(\"c\", 1.0, \"2026-03-12T00:00:00+00:00\"),\n        ];\n        apply_sort(&mut results, SortOrder::Oldest);\n        assert_eq!(results[0].session_id, \"a\");\n        assert_eq!(results[1].session_id, \"c\");\n        assert_eq!(results[2].session_id, \"b\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_output_json_serialization() {\n        let output = SearchOutput test_search_result_json_roundtrip() {\n        let result = SearchResult {\n            session_id: \"aider/def456\".to_string(),\n            source_agent: \"aider\".to_string(),\n            project: None,\n            timestamp: None,\n            turns: Some(5),\n            outcome: None,\n            score: 3.0,\n            summary: None,\n            snippet: Some(\"some code snippet\".to_string()),\n            tags: vec![\"python\".to_string(), \"docker\".to_string()],\n            doc_type: Some(\"session\".to_string()),\n            model: None,\n            token_count: 3,\n        };\n        let json = serde_json::to_string(&result).unwrap();\n        let deser: SearchResult = serde_json::from_str(&json).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(deser.session_id, SessionManifest};\n        use crate::index::build_session_document;\n        use tempfile::TempDir;\n\n        let temp_dir = TempDir::new().unwrap();\n        let index_path = temp_dir.path().join(\"index\").join(\"tantivy\");\n        let (schema, fields) = build_schema();\n        std::fs::create_dir_all(&index_path).unwrap();\n        let index = tantivy::Index::create_in_dir(&index_path, schema).unwrap();\n        let mut writer = index.writer(50_000_000).unwrap();\n\n        let now = Utc::now();\n\n        // Session with a specific error fingerprint (stored lowercase for consistent matching)\n        let mut m1 = SessionManifest::new(\"claude/err-1\".to_string(), \"claude-code\".to_string());\n        m1.started = now;\n        m1.turns = 2;\n        let e1 = vec![\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"claude/err-1\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::User,\n                \"help fix this error\".to_string(),\n            ),\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"claude/err-1\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::ToolResult,\n                \"connection refused error\".to_string(),\n            )\n            .with_error_fingerprints(vec![\"connectionrefusederror\".to_string()]),\n        ];\n        writer\n            .add_document(build_session_document(&fields, &e1, &m1))\n            .unwrap();\n\n        // Session without the error fingerprint\n        let mut m2 = SessionManifest::new(\"claude/noerr\".to_string(), \"claude-code\".to_string());\n        m2.started = now;\n        m2.turns = 2;\n        let e2 = vec![\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"claude/noerr\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::User,\n                \"normal session\".to_string(),\n            ),\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"claude/noerr\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::Assistant,\n                \"done\".to_string(),\n            ),\n        ];\n        writer\n            .add_document(build_session_document(&fields, &e2, &m2))\n            .unwrap();\n        writer.commit().unwrap();\n        writer.wait_merging_threads().unwrap();\n\n        let opts = SearchOptions {\n            query: None,\n            error_pattern: Some(\"connectionrefusederror\".to_string()),\n            ..default_opts()\n        };\n        let result = execute_search(temp_dir.path(), &opts).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.results.len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(result.results[0].session_id, \"claude/err-1\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_integration_code_search() {\n        use crate::event::{Event, Role, SessionManifest};\n        use crate::index::{build_code_artifact_document, build_session_document};\n        use tempfile::TempDir;\n\n        let temp_dir = TempDir::new().unwrap();\n        let index_path = temp_dir.path().join(\"index\").join(\"tantivy\");\n        let (schema, fields) = build_schema();\n        std::fs::create_dir_all(&index_path).unwrap();\n        let index = tantivy::Index::create_in_dir(&index_path, schema).unwrap();\n        let mut writer = index.writer(50_000_000).unwrap();\n\n        let now = Utc::now();\n\n        // Session document\n        let m = SessionManifest::new(\"claude/code-1\".to_string(), \"claude-code\".to_string());\n        let e = vec![Event::new(\n            now,\n            \"claude/code-1\".to_string(),\n            \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n            Role::User,\n            \"write a function\".to_string(),\n        )];\n        writer\n            .add_document(build_session_document(&fields, &e, &m))\n            .unwrap();\n\n        // Code artifact document\n        let code_doc = build_code_artifact_document(\n            &fields,\n            \"claude/code-1\",\n            \"claude-code\",\n            Some(\"/home/user/project\"),\n            now,\n            \"rust\",\n            \"src/auth.rs\",\n            \"fn authenticate(user: &User) -> bool { validate_token(user.token) }\",\n            true,\n            None,\n        );\n        writer.add_document(code_doc).unwrap();\n        writer.commit().unwrap();\n        writer.wait_merging_threads().unwrap();\n\n        let opts = &opts).unwrap();\n        assert!(result.total_matches >= 1);\n        // All results should be code_artifact type (code search filters to that doc_type)\n        for r in &result.results {\n            assert_eq!(r.doc_type.as_deref(), Some(\"code_artifact\"));\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_integration_code_search_with_lang_filter() {\n        use crate::index::build_code_artifact_document;\n        use tempfile::TempDir;\n\n        let temp_dir = TempDir::new().unwrap();\n        let index_path = temp_dir.path().join(\"index\").join(\"tantivy\");\n        let (schema, fields) = build_schema();\n        std::fs::create_dir_all(&index_path).unwrap();\n        let index = tantivy::Index::create_in_dir(&index_path, schema).unwrap();\n        let mut writer = index.writer(50_000_000).unwrap();\n\n        let now = Utc::now();\n\n        // Rust code artifact\n        writer\n            .add_document(build_code_artifact_document(\n                &fields,\n                \"claude/rust-s\",\n                \"claude-code\",\n                None,\n                now,\n                \"rust\",\n                \"src/main.rs\",\n                \"fn handle_request(req: &Request) -> Response { process(req) }\",\n                true,\n                None,\n            ))\n            .unwrap();\n\n        // Python code artifact\n        writer\n            .add_document(build_code_artifact_document(\n                &fields,\n                \"claude/py-s\",\n                \"claude-code\",\n                None,\n                now,\n                \"python\",\n                \"app/handler.py\",\n                \"def handle_request(req): return process(req)\",\n                true,\n                None,\n            ))\n            .unwrap();\n        writer.commit().unwrap();\n        writer.wait_merging_threads().unwrap();\n\n        // Filter by rust language\n        let opts = SearchOptions {\n            query: None,\n            code_query: Some(\"handle_request\".to_string()),\n            code_lang: Some(\"rust\".to_string()),\n            ..default_opts()\n        };\n        let result = execute_search(temp_dir.path(), &opts).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.results.len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(result.results[0].session_id, \"claude/rust-s\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_integration_combined_filters() {\n        // agent + outcome + tag all applied together (AND logic)\n        use crate::event::{Event, Role, SessionManifest};\n        use crate::index::build_session_document;\n        use tempfile::TempDir;\n\n        let temp_dir = TempDir::new().unwrap();\n        let index_path = temp_dir.path().join(\"index\").join(\"tantivy\");\n        let (schema, fields) = build_schema();\n        std::fs::create_dir_all(&index_path).unwrap();\n        let index = tantivy::Index::create_in_dir(&index_path, schema).unwrap();\n        let mut writer = index.writer(50_000_000).unwrap();\n\n        let now = Utc::now();\n\n        // Target: claude-code + success + rust tag\n        let mut m1 = SessionManifest::new(\"claude/target\".to_string(), \"claude-code\".to_string());\n        m1.outcome = Some(\"success\".to_string());\n        m1.tags = vec![\"rust\".to_string()];\n        m1.started = now;\n        m1.turns = 2;\n        let e1 = vec![\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"claude/target\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::User,\n                \"fix memory leak\".to_string(),\n            ),\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"claude/target\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::Assistant,\n                \"fixed it\".to_string(),\n            ),\n        ];\n        writer\n            .add_document(build_session_document(&fields, &e1, &m1))\n            .unwrap();\n\n        // Wrong outcome (failure instead of success)\n        let mut m2 = SessionManifest::new(\n            \"claude/wrong-outcome\".to_string(),\n            \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n        );\n        m2.outcome = Some(\"failure\".to_string());\n        m2.tags = vec![\"rust\".to_string()];\n        m2.started = now;\n        m2.turns = 2;\n        let e2 = vec![\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"claude/wrong-outcome\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::User,\n                \"fix memory leak\".to_string(),\n            ),\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"claude/wrong-outcome\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::Assistant,\n                \"failed\".to_string(),\n            ),\n        ];\n        writer\n            .add_document(build_session_document(&fields, &e2, &m2))\n            .unwrap();\n\n        // Wrong agent (aider instead of claude-code)\n        let mut m3 = SessionManifest::new(\"aider/wrong-agent\".to_string(), \"aider\".to_string());\n        m3.outcome = Some(\"success\".to_string());\n        m3.tags = vec![\"rust\".to_string()];\n        m3.started = now;\n        m3.turns = 2;\n        let e3 = vec![\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"aider/wrong-agent\".to_string(),\n                \"aider\".to_string(),\n                Role::User,\n                \"fix memory leak\".to_string(),\n            ),\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"aider/wrong-agent\".to_string(),\n                \"aider\".to_string(),\n                Role::Assistant,\n                \"fixed\".to_string(),\n            ),\n        ];\n        writer\n            .add_document(build_session_document(&fields, &e3, &m3))\n            .unwrap();\n\n        // Wrong tag (python instead of rust)\n        let mut m4 =\n            SessionManifest::new(\"claude/wrong-tag\".to_string(), \"claude-code\".to_string());\n        m4.outcome = Some(\"success\".to_string());\n        m4.tags = vec![\"python\".to_string()];\n        m4.started = now;\n        m4.turns = 2;\n        let e4 = vec![\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"claude/wrong-tag\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::User,\n                \"fix memory leak\".to_string(),\n            ),\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"claude/wrong-tag\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::Assistant,\n                \"fixed\".to_string(),\n            ),\n        ];\n        writer\n            .add_document(build_session_document(&fields, &e4, &m4))\n            .unwrap();\n\n        writer.commit().unwrap();\n        writer.wait_merging_threads().unwrap();\n\n        let opts = SearchOptions {\n            query: Some(\"fix memory\".to_string()),\n            agent: vec![\"claude-code\".to_string()],\n            outcome: Some(\"success\".to_string()),\n            tag: vec![\"rust\".to_string()],\n            ..default_opts()\n        };\n        let result = execute_search(temp_dir.path(), &opts).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.results.len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(result.results[0].session_id, \"claude/target\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_integration_unicode_content() {\n        use crate::event::{Event, Role, SessionManifest};\n tantivy::Index::create_in_dir(&index_path, schema).unwrap();\n        let mut writer = index.writer(50_000_000).unwrap();\n\n        let now = Utc::now();\n        let mut m = SessionManifest::new(\"claude/unicode\".to_string(), \"claude-code\".to_string());\n        m.started = now;\n        m.turns = 2;\n        m.summary = Some(\"Unicode test session with authentication\".to_string());\n        let e = vec![\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"claude/unicode\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::User,\n                \"Fix authentication bug in the system\".to_string(),\n            ),\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"claude/unicode\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::Assistant,\n                \"Fixed authentication: 日本語コメント and Ответ на русском\".to_string(),\n std::fs::create_dir_all(&index_path).unwrap();\n        let index = tantivy::Index::create_in_dir(&index_path, schema).unwrap();\n        let mut writer = index.writer(50_000_000).unwrap();\n\n     let mut m_normal =\n            SessionManifest::new(\"claude/normal\".to_string(), \"claude-code\".to_string());\n        m_normal.started = now;\n        m_normal.turns = 1;\n        let ..default_opts()\n        };\n        let result = execute_search(temp_dir.path(), &opts).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.results.len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(result.results[0].session_id,    use tempfile::TempDir;\n\n        let temp_dir = TempDir::new().unwrap();\n        let index_path = temp_dir.path().join(\"index\").join(\"tantivy\");\n        let (schema, fields) = build_schema();\n        std::fs::create_dir_all(&index_path).unwrap();\n        let index = tantivy::Index::create_in_dir(&index_path, schema).unwrap();\n        let mut writer = index.writer(50_000_000).unwrap();\n\n        let now = Utc::now();\n\n        for i in 0..3 {\n            let session_id = format!(\"claude/session-{}\", i);\n            let mut m = SessionManifest::new(session_id.clone(), \"claude-code\".to_string());\n            m.started = now - chrono::Duration::hours(i as i64);\n            m.turns = 1;\n            let e = vec![Event::new(\n                now,\n                session_id.clone(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::User,\n                \"debug the authentication code\".to_string(),\n            )];\n            writer\n                .add_document(build_session_document(&fields, &e, &m))\n                .unwrap();\n        }\n        writer.commit().unwrap();\n        writer.wait_merging_threads().unwrap();\n\n        let opts_all = SearchOptions {\n            query: Some(\"authentication\".to_string()),\n            max_results: 10,\n            offset: 0,\n            ..default_opts()\n        };\n        let all_results = execute_search(temp_dir.path(), &opts_all).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(all_results.results.len(), 3);\n\n        let opts_offset = SearchOptions {\n            query: Some(\"authentication\".to_string()),\n            max_results: 10,\n            offset: 1,\n            ..default_opts()\n        };\n        let offset_results = execute_search(temp_dir.path(), &opts_offset).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(offset_results.results.len(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(\n            offset_results.results[0].session_id,\n            all_results.results[1].session_id\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_integration_multiple_tag_filters() {\n        // Multiple (schema, fields) = build_schema();\n        std::fs::create_dir_all(&index_path).unwrap();\n        let index = tantivy::Index::create_in_dir(&index_path, schema).unwrap();\n        let mut writer = function to validate input data\".to_string(),\n        )];\n        writer\n            .add_document(build_session_document(&fields, &e, &m))\n            .unwrap();\n\n        writer\n            .add_document(build_code_artifact_document(\n                &fields,\n                \"claude/1\",\n                \"claude-code\",\n                None,\n                now,\n &opts).unwrap();\n        for r in &result.results {\n            assert_eq!(r.doc_type.as_deref(), Some(\"session\"));\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_integration_very_large_session() {\n        // Very large sessions should be indexed and searched without panic\n        use crate::event::{Event, Role, SessionManifest};\n        use crate::index::build_session_document;\n        use tempfile::TempDir;\n\n        let temp_dir = TempDir::new().unwrap();\n        let index_path = temp_dir.path().join(\"index\").join(\"tantivy\");\n        let (schema, fields) = build_schema();\n        std::fs::create_dir_all(&index_path).unwrap();\n        let index = tantivy::Index::create_in_dir(&index_path, schema).unwrap();\n        let mut writer = index.writer(50_000_000).unwrap();\n\n        let now = Utc::now();\n        let mut m = SessionManifest::new(\"claude/large\".to_string(), \"claude-code\".to_string());\n        m.started = now;\n        m.turns = 3;\n\n        // Very large tool result (will trigger content truncation)\n        let large_tool_result = \"this is a large tool result with lots of content \".repeat(15000);\n        let e = vec![\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"claude/large\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::User,\n                \"analyze this large codebase for authentication issues\".to_string(),\n            ),\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"claude/large\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::ToolResult,\n                large_tool_result,\n            ),\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"claude/large\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::Assistant,\n                \"I analyzed the authentication code and found issues\".to_string(),\n            ),\n        ];\n        writer\n            .add_document(build_session_document(&fields, &e, &m))\n            .unwrap();\n        writer.commit().unwrap();\n        writer.wait_merging_threads().unwrap();\n\n        let opts = SearchOptions {\n            query: Some(\"authentication\".to_string()),\n            ..default_opts()\n        };\n        let result = execute_search(temp_dir.path(), &opts).unwrap();\n        assert!(result.total_matches >= 1);\n        assert_eq!(result.results[0].session_id, \"claude/large\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_integration_aider_session_roundtrip() {\n        // Full scrape → index → search roundtrip for a simulated Aider session\n        use crate::event::{Event, Role, SessionManifest};\n        use crate::index::build_session_document;\n        use tempfile::TempDir;\n\n        let temp_dir = TempDir::new().unwrap();\n        let index_path = tantivy::Index::create_in_dir(&index_path, schema).unwrap();\n        let mut writer = index.writer(50_000_000).unwrap();\n\n        let now = Utc::now();\n        let mut m = SessionManifest::new(\"aider/session-1\".to_string(), \"aider\".to_string());\n        m.project = Some(\"/home/user/api\".to_string());\n        m.started = now;\n        m.turns = 5;\n        m.summary = Some(\"Refactored authentication middleware using JWT tokens\".to_string());\n        m.outcome = Some(\"success\".to_string());\n        m.tags = vec![\n            \"python\".to_string(),\n            \"jwt\".to_string(),\n            \"authentication\".to_string(),\n        ];\n        let e = vec![\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"aider/session-1\".to_string(),\n                \"aider\".to_string(),\n                Role::User,\n                \"Refactor the auth middleware to use JWT tokens instead of session cookies\"\n                    .to_string(),\n            ),\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"aider/session-1\".to_string(),\n                \"aider\".to_string(),\n                Role::Assistant,\n                \"I'll update the middleware to use JWT. Plan: 1) Install PyJWT 2) Update auth.py\"\n                    .to_string(),\n            ),\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"aider/session-1\".to_string(),\n                \"aider\".to_string(),\n                Role::ToolCall,\n                \"pip install PyJWT\".to_string(),\n            )\n            .with_tool(Some(\"Bash\".to_string())),\n PyJWT-2.8.0\".to_string(),\n            ),\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"aider/session-1\".to_string(),\n                \"aider\".to_string(),\n                Role::Assistant,\n                \"JWT authentication has been implemented successfully.\".to_string(),\n            ),\n        ];\n        writer\n            .add_document(build_session_document(&fields, &e, &m))\n            .unwrap();\n        writer.commit().unwrap();\n        writer.wait_merging_threads().unwrap();\n\n        let opts = SearchOptions {\n            query: Some(\"JWT authentication\".to_string()),\n            agent: vec![\"aider\".to_string()],\n            ..default_opts()\n        };\n        let result = execute_search(temp_dir.path(), &opts).unwrap();\n        assert!(result.total_matches >= 1);\n        assert_eq!(result.results[0].session_id, \"aider/session-1\");\n        assert_eq!(result.results[0].source_agent, \"aider\");\n        assert!(result.results[0].summary.is_some());\n        assert_eq!(result.results[0].outcome.as_deref(), Some(\"success\"));\n    }\n\n Event::new(now, \"claude/session-2\".to_string(), \"claude-code\".to_string(), Role::Assistant,\n                \"Rate limiting has been added to the authentication endpoints.\".to_string()),\n        ];\n        writer\n            .add_document(build_session_document(&fields, &e, &m))\n            .unwrap();\n        writer.commit().unwrap();\n        writer.wait_merging_threads().unwrap();\n\n    use crate::index::build_session_document;\n        use tempfile::TempDir;\n\n        let temp_dir = TempDir::new().unwrap();\n        let index_path = temp_dir.path().join(\"index\").join(\"tantivy\");\n        let (schema, fields) = build_schema();\n        std::fs::create_dir_all(&index_path).unwrap();\n        let index = tantivy::Index::create_in_dir(&index_path, schema).unwrap();\n        let mut writer = index.writer(50_000_000).unwrap();\n\n        let now = Utc::now();\n\n        // Session with solution_summary\n        let mut m = SessionManifest::new(\"claude/solved\".to_string(), \"claude-code\".to_string());\n        m.started = now;\n        m.turns = 3;\n        m.summary = Some(\"Investigated memory leak problem\".to_string());\n        // solution_summary is not in SessionManifest — set it via the document directly\n        // We'll test that --solution_only routes snippet to the solution field\n        let e = vec![\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"claude/solved\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::User,\n                \"fix the memory leak in the allocator\".to_string(),\n            ),\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"claude/solved\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::Assistant,\n                \"memory leak fixed by patching the allocator\".to_string(),\n            ),\n        ];\n        writer\n            .add_document(build_session_document(&fields, &e, &m))\n            .unwrap();\n        writer.commit().unwrap();\n        writer.wait_merging_threads().unwrap();\n\n        // --solution_only should still return results if query matches content\n        let opts = SearchOptions {\n            query: Some(\"memory leak\".to_string()),\n            solution_only: true,\n            ..default_opts()\n        };\n        let result = execute_search(temp_dir.path(), &opts).unwrap();\n        // Results are valid; solution_only only affects snippet source and adds a Should boost\n        // result.total_matches may be 0 if solution_summary is empty — that's valid\n        let _ = result.total_matches;\n        // The important thing is no panic/error\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_integration_token_budget() {\n        // Verifies the end-to-end token_budget knapsack flow through execute_search:\n        // - results fit within the budget\n        // - token_count is populated on each result\n        // - highest-relevance results are preferred\n        use crate::event::{Event, Role, SessionManifest};\n        use crate::index::build_session_document;\n        use tempfile::TempDir;\n\n        let temp_dir = TempDir::new().unwrap();\n        let index_path = temp_dir.path().join(\"index\").join(\"tantivy\");\n        let (schema, fields) = build_schema();\n        std::fs::create_dir_all(&index_path).unwrap();\n        let index = tantivy::Index::create_in_dir(&index_path, schema).unwrap();\n        let mut writer = index.writer(50_000_000).unwrap();\n\n        let now = Utc::now();\n\n        // Add three sessions with the same query term so they all match\n        for (i, session_id) in [\"claude/tb-1\", \"claude/tb-2\", \"claude/tb-3\"]\n            .iter()\n            .enumerate()\n        {\n            let mut m = SessionManifest::new(session_id.to_string(), \"claude-code\".to_string());\n            m.started = now - chrono::Duration::days(i as i64);\n            m.turns = 5;\n            m.summary = Some(format!(\"session {} summary for knapsack test\", i + 1));\n            let e = vec![\n                Event::new(\n                    now,\n                    session_id.to_string(),\n                    \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                    Role::User,\n                    \"knapsack budget test query\".to_string(),\n                ),\n                Event::new(\n                    now,\n                    session_id.to_string(),\n                    \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                    Role::Assistant,\n                    format!(\"response content for session {}\", i + 1),\n                ),\n            ];\n            writer\n                .add_document(build_session_document(&fields, &e, &m))\n                .unwrap();\n        }\n        writer.commit().unwrap();\n        writer.wait_merging_threads().unwrap();\n\n        // Small budget — should include at least one result but stay within budget\n        let budget = 50;\n        let opts = SearchOptions {\n            query: Some(\"knapsack budget test\".to_string()),\n            token_budget: Some(budget),\n            snippet_length: 200,\n            max_results: 10,\n            ..default_opts()\n        };\n        let result = execute_search(temp_dir.path(), &opts).unwrap();\n\n        // At least one result returned\n        assert!(\n            !result.results.is_empty(),\n            \"should return at least one result\"\n        );\n\n        // Total token_count must not exceed budget\n        let total_tokens: usize = result.results.iter().map(|r| r.token_count).sum();\n        assert!(\n            total_tokens <= budget,\n            \"total token_count {} exceeded budget {}\",\n            total_tokens,\n            budget\n        );\n\n        // Every result must have token_count > 0\n        for r in &result.results {\n            assert!(\n                r.token_count > 0,\n                \"token_count should be > 0 for result {}\",\n                r.session_id\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_integration_file_path_filter() {\n        use crate::event::{Event, Role, SessionManifest};\n        use crate::index::build_session_document;\n        use tempfile::TempDir;\n\n        let temp_dir = TempDir::new().unwrap();\n        let index_path = temp_dir.path().join(\"index\").join(\"tantivy\");\n        let (schema, fields) = build_schema();\n        std::fs::create_dir_all(&index_path).unwrap();\n        let index = tantivy::Index::create_in_dir(&index_path, schema).unwrap();\n        let mut writer = index.writer(50_000_000).unwrap();\n\n        let now = Utc::now();\n\n        // Session 1: touches src/auth.rs\n        let mut m1 = SessionManifest::new(\"claude/auth-1\".to_string(), \"claude-code\".to_string());\n        m1.started = now;\n        m1.turns = 2;\n        m1.files_touched = vec![\"src/auth.rs\".to_string()];\n        let e1 = vec![\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"claude/auth-1\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::User,\n                \"fix the authentication bug\".to_string(),\n            ),\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"claude/auth-1\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::Assistant,\n                \"fixed the auth issue\".to_string(),\n            ),\n        ];\n        writer\n            .add_document(build_session_document(&fields, &e1, &m1))\n            .unwrap();\n\n        // Session 2: touches a different file\n        let mut m2 = SessionManifest::new(\"claude/db-1\".to_string(), \"claude-code\".to_string());\n        m2.started = now;\n        m2.turns = 2;\n        m2.files_touched = vec![\"src/database.rs\".to_string()];\n        let e2 = vec![\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"claude/db-1\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::User,\n                \"fix the authentication bug\".to_string(),\n            ),\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"claude/db-1\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::Assistant,\n                \"fixed the auth issue in db\".to_string(),\n            ),\n        ];\n        writer\n            .add_document(build_session_document(&fields, &e2, &m2))\n            .unwrap();\n        writer.commit().unwrap();\n        writer.wait_merging_threads().unwrap();\n\n        // Filter by file path — should only return the session that touched auth.rs\n        let opts = SearchOptions {\n            query: Some(\"authentication\".to_string()),\n            file_path: Some(\"src/auth.rs\".to_string()),\n            ..default_opts()\n        };\n        let result = execute_search(temp_dir.path(), &opts).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.results.len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(result.results[0].session_id, \"claude/auth-1\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_integration_auto_fuzzy_fallback() {\n        // When an exact search returns 0 results, the engine automatically retries with fuzzy.\n        use crate::event::{Event, Role, SessionManifest};\n        use crate::index::build_session_document;\n        use tempfile::TempDir;\n\n        let temp_dir = TempDir::new().unwrap();\n        let index_path = temp_dir.path().join(\"index\").join(\"tantivy\");\n        let (schema, fields) = build_schema();\n        std::fs::create_dir_all(&index_path).unwrap();\n        let index = tantivy::Index::create_in_dir(&index_path, schema).unwrap();\n        let mut writer = index.writer(50_000_000).unwrap();\n\n        let now = Utc::now();\n        let mut m = SessionManifest::new(\"claude/k8s\".to_string(), \"claude-code\".to_string());\n        m.started = now;\n        m.turns = 2;\n        let e = vec![\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"claude/k8s\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::User,\n                \"deploy the kubernetes cluster\".to_string(),\n            ),\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"claude/k8s\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::Assistant,\n                \"deployed kubernetes successfully\".to_string(),\n            ),\n        ];\n        writer\n            .add_document(build_session_document(&fields, &e, &m))\n            .unwrap();\n        writer.commit().unwrap();\n        writer.wait_merging_threads().unwrap();\n\n        // Misspelling \"kuberntes\" (missing 'e') won't match exact BM25 but triggers fuzzy fallback\n        let opts = SearchOptions {\n            query: Some(\"kuberntes\".to_string()),\n            fuzzy: false, // explicit fuzzy off — relies on automatic fallback\n            ..default_opts()\n        };\n        let result = execute_search(temp_dir.path(), &opts).unwrap();\n        assert!(\n            result.fuzzy_fallback,\n            \"expected automatic fuzzy fallback to be triggered\"\n        );\n        assert!(\n            result.total_matches >= 1,\n            \"fuzzy fallback should return at least 1 result\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_format_human_with_fuzzy_fallback() {\n        // The \"[fuzzy fallback]\" note must appear in human output when fuzzy_fallback=true.\n        let output = SearchOutput {\n            query: \"kuberntes\".to_string(),\n            total_matches: 0,\n            search_time_ms: 3,\n            sessions_searched: 10,\n            results: vec![],\n            fuzzy_fallback: true,\n        };\n        let formatted = format_human(&output, 200);\n        assert!(\n            formatted.contains(\"[fuzzy fallback]\"),\n            \"human output should note fuzzy fallback\"\n        );\n        assert!(formatted.contains(\"kuberntes\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_is_stop_word() {\n        // Common English stop words must be detected.\n        assert!(is_stop_word(\"the\"));\n        assert!(is_stop_word(\"and\"));\n        assert!(is_stop_word(\"with\"));\n        assert!(is_stop_word(\"was\"));\n        assert!(is_stop_word(\"this\"));\n        // Tech terms must NOT be stop words.\n        assert!(!is_stop_word(\"kubernetes\"));\n        assert!(!is_stop_word(\"postgres\"));\n        assert!(!is_stop_word(\"authentication\"));\n        assert!(!is_stop_word(\"migration\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_integration_solution_only_snippet_source() {\n        // When solution_only=true, the snippet is drawn from solution_summary, not content.\n        use crate::event::{Event, Role, SessionManifest};\n        use crate::index::build_session_document;\n        use tempfile::TempDir;\n\n        let temp_dir = TempDir::new().unwrap();\n        let index_path = temp_dir.path().join(\"index\").join(\"tantivy\");\n        let (schema, fields) = build_schema();\n        std::fs::create_dir_all(&index_path).unwrap();\n        let index = tantivy::Index::create_in_dir(&index_path, schema).unwrap();\n        let mut writer = index.writer(50_000_000).unwrap();\n\n        let now = Utc::now();\n\n        let mut m = SessionManifest::new(\"claude/sol\".to_string(), \"claude-code\".to_string());\n        m.started = now;\n        m.turns = 2;\n        let e = vec![\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"claude/sol\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::User,\n                \"fix concurrency and authentication issue\".to_string(),\n            ),\n            Event::new(\n                now,\n                \"claude/sol\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::Assistant,\n                \"analyzing the authentication code\".to_string(),\n            ),\n        ];\n        // Build the session doc and manually attach a solution_summary\n        let mut doc = build_session_document(&fields, &e, &m);\n        doc.add_text(\n            fields.solution_summary,\n            \"SOLUTION: use stateless JWT tokens with 1h expiry for authentication\",\n        );\n        writer.add_document(doc).unwrap();\n        writer.commit().unwrap();\n        writer.wait_merging_threads().unwrap();\n\n        let opts = SearchOptions {\n            query: Some(\"authentication\".to_string()),\n            solution_only: true,\n            snippet_length: 500,\n            ..default_opts()\n        };\n        let result = execute_search(temp_dir.path(), &opts).unwrap();\n        assert!(\n            result.total_matches >= 1,\n            \"session should be found via content\"\n        );\n        let r = &result.results[0];\n        assert_eq!(r.session_id, \"claude/sol\");\n        // Snippet must come from solution_summary (contains \"SOLUTION\" or \"JWT\")\n        if let Some(ref snippet) = r.snippet {\n            assert!(\n                snippet.contains(\"SOLUTION\") || snippet.contains(\"JWT\") || snippet.contains(\"jwt\"),\n                \"solution_only snippet should come from solution_summary field, got: {}\",\n                snippet\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_integration_before_filter_standalone() {\n        // --before alone excludes sessions after the cutoff date.\n        use crate::event::{Event, Role, SessionManifest};\n        use crate::index::build_session_document;\n        use tempfile::TempDir;\n\n        let temp_dir = TempDir::new().unwrap();\n        let index_path = temp_dir.path().join(\"index\").join(\"tantivy\");\n        let (schema, fields) = build_schema();\n        std::fs::create_dir_all(&index_path).unwrap();\n        let index = tantivy::Index::create_in_dir(&index_path, schema).unwrap();\n        let mut writer = index.writer(50_000_000).unwrap();\n\n        let now = Utc::now();\n\n        let mut m_old = SessionManifest::new(\"old/1\".to_string(), \"test\".to_string());\n        m_old.started = now - chrono::Duration::days(10);\n        m_old.turns = 1;\n        let e_old = vec![Event::new(\n            now - chrono::Duration::days(10),\n            \"old/1\".to_string(),\n            \"test\".to_string(),\n            Role::User,\n            \"historical session content\".to_string(),\n        )];\n        writer\n            .add_document(build_session_document(&fields, &e_old, &m_old))\n            .unwrap();\n\n        let mut m_new = SessionManifest::new(\"new/1\".to_string(), \"test\".to_string());\n        m_new.started = now - chrono::Duration::hours(1);\n        m_new.turns = 1;\n        let e_new = vec![Event::new(\n            now - chrono::Duration::hours(1),\n            \"new/1\".to_string(),\n            \"test\".to_string(),\n            Role::User,\n            \"historical session content\".to_string(),\n        )];\n        writer\n            .add_document(build_session_document(&fields, &e_new, &m_new))\n            .unwrap();\n        writer.commit().unwrap();\n        writer.wait_merging_threads().unwrap();\n\n        // --before 2d: should exclude the recent session\n        let opts = SearchOptions {\n            query: Some(\"historical\".to_string()),\n            before: Some(now - chrono::Duration::days(2)),\n            ..default_opts()\n        };\n        let result = execute_search(temp_dir.path(), &opts).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.results.len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(result.results[0].session_id, \"old/1\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_integration_single_agent_filter() {\n        // Single     let temp_dir = TempDir::new().unwrap();\n        let index_path = temp_dir.path().join(\"index\").join(\"tantivy\");\n        let (schema, fields) = build_schema();\n   )];\n        writer\n            .add_document(build_session_document(&fields, &e1, &m1))\n            .unwrap();\n\n        let mut m2 = SessionManifest::new(\"aider/1\".to_string(), Some(\"code\".to_string()),\n            agent: vec![\"aider\".to_string()],\n            ..default_opts()\n        };\n        let result = execute_search(temp_dir.path(), &opts).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(result.results.len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(result.results[0].source_agent, \"aider\");\n    }\n\n    // --- Phase 2 integration tests ---\n\n    /// Helper: build a multi-session index with both Claude Code and Aider sessions.\n    /// Returns the TempDir (must be kept alive for the test duration).\n    fn build_multi_session_index() -> (tempfile::TempDir, IndexFields) {\n        use crate::event::{Event, Role, SessionManifest};\n        use crate::index::build_session_document;\n        use tempfile::TempDir;\n\n        let temp_dir = TempDir::new().unwrap();\n        let index_path = temp_dir.path().join(\"index\").join(\"tantivy\");\n        let (schema, fields) = build_schema();\n        std::fs::create_dir_all(&index_path).unwrap();\n        let index = tantivy::Index::create_in_dir(&index_path, schema).unwrap();\n        let mut writer = index.writer(50_000_000).unwrap();\n\n        let now = Utc::now();\n\n        // Session 1: Claude Code — recent, success, rust/docker, auth topic\n        let mut m1 = SessionManifest::new(\"claude/auth-1\".to_string(), \"claude-code\".to_string());\n        m1.project = Some(\"/home/user/myapp\".to_string());\n        m1.started = now - chrono::Duration::hours(2);\n        m1.turns = 6;\n        m1.summary = Some(\"Added JWT authentication middleware\".to_string());\n        m1.outcome = Some(\"success\".to_string());\n        m1.tags = vec![\"rust\".to_string(), \"docker\".to_string()];\n        m1.model = Some(\"claude-sonnet-4-5\".to_string());\n        m1.files_touched = vec![\"src/auth.rs\".to_string(), \"src/middleware.rs\".to_string()];\n        let e1 = vec![\n            Event::new(\n                now - chrono::Duration::hours(2),\n                \"claude/auth-1\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::User,\n                \"Add JWT authentication to the middleware\".to_string(),\n            ),\n            Event::new(\n                now - chrono::Duration::hours(2),\n                \"claude/auth-1\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::Assistant,\n                \"I'll implement JWT auth. Let me read the middleware first.\".to_string(),\n            ),\n            Event::new(\n                now - chrono::Duration::hours(2),\n                \"claude/auth-1\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::ToolCall,\n                \"src/middleware.rs\".to_string(),\n            )\n            .with_tool(Some(\"Read\".to_string()))\n            .with_file_paths(vec![\"src/middleware.rs\".to_string()]),\n            Event::new(\n                now - chrono::Duration::hours(2),\n                \"claude/auth-1\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::ToolResult,\n                \"pub fn handle_request(req: &Request) -> Response { /* ... */ }\".to_string(),\n            ),\n            Event::new(\n                now - chrono::Duration::hours(2),\n                \"claude/auth-1\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::ToolCall,\n                \"```rust\\nfn validate_jwt(token: &str) -> bool { true }\\n```\".to_string(),\n            )\n            .with_tool(Some(\"Edit\".to_string()))\n            .with_file_paths(vec![\"src/auth.rs\".to_string()]),\n            Event::new(\n                now - chrono::Duration::hours(2),\n                \"claude/auth-1\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::Assistant,\n                \"JWT authentication middleware implemented successfully.\".to_string(),\n            ),\n        ];\n        writer\n            .add_document(build_session_document(&fields, &e1, &m1))\n            .unwrap();\n\n        // Session 2: Claude Code — older, failure, postgres migration\n        let mut m2 =\n            SessionManifest::new(\"claude/pg-migration\".to_string(), \"claude-code\".to_string());\n        m2.project = Some(\"/home/user/api-server\".to_string());\n        m2.started = now - chrono::Duration::days(5);\n        m2.turns = 4;\n        m2.summary =\n            Some(\"Failed to migrate postgres schema — downtime window too short\".to_string());\n        m2.outcome = Some(\"failure\".to_string());\n        m2.tags = vec![\"postgres\".to_string(), \"migration\".to_string()];\n        m2.model = Some(\"claude-opus-4-6\".to_string());\n        m2.files_touched = vec![\"migrations/v3.sql\".to_string()];\n        let e2 = vec![\n            Event::new(\n                now - chrono::Duration::days(5),\n                \"claude/pg-migration\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::User,\n                \"Migrate the postgres schema from v3 to v4\".to_string(),\n            ),\n            Event::new(\n                now - chrono::Duration::days(5),\n                \"claude/pg-migration\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::Assistant,\n                \"I'll create the migration script for postgres.\".to_string(),\n            ),\n            Event::new(\n                now - chrono::Duration::days(5),\n                \"claude/pg-migration\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::ToolResult,\n                \"connection refused error\".to_string(),\n            )\n            .with_error_fingerprints(vec![\"ConnectionRefusedError\".to_string()]),\n            Event::new(\n                now - chrono::Duration::days(5),\n                \"claude/pg-migration\".to_string(),\n                \"claude-code\".to_string(),\n                Role::Assistant,\n                \"The postgres migration failed due to connection timeout.\".to_string(),\n            ),\n        ];\n        writer\n            .add_document(build_session_document(&fields, &e2, &m2))\n            .unwrap();\n\n        // Session 3: Aider — recent, success, python/jwt\n        let mut m3 = SessionManifest::new(\"aider/jwt-refactor\".to_string(), \"aider\".to_string());\n        m3.project = Some(\"/home/user/api-server\".to_string());\n        m3.started = now - chrono::Duration::hours(1);\n        m3.turns = 5;\n        m3.summary = Some(\"Refactored authentication middleware using JWT tokens\".to_string());\n        m3.outcome = Some(\"success\".to_string());\n        m3.tags = vec![\n            \"python\".to_string(),\n            \"jwt\".to_string(),\n            \"authentication\".to_string(),\n        ];\n        m3.files_touched = vec![\"app/auth.py\".to_string()];\n        let e3 = vec![\n            Event::new(\n                now - chrono::Duration::hours(1),\n                \"aider/jwt-refactor\".to_string(),\n                \"aider\".to_string(),\n                Role::User,\n                \"Refactor auth middleware to use JWT tokens instead of session cookies\".to_string(),\n            ),\n            Event::new(\n                now - Session 4: Aider — old, success, css/frontend\n        let mut m4 = SessionManifest::new(\"aider/css-fix\".to_string(), \"aider\".to_string());\n        m4.project = \"Fix the CSS flexbox layout for mobile\".to_string(),\n            ),\n            Event::new(\n                now - chrono::Duration::days(10),\n                \"aider/css-fix\".to_string(),\n                \"aider\".to_string(),\n                Role::Assistant,\n                \"Updated CSS grid and flexbox properties.\".to_string(),\n            ),\n            Event::new(\n responsive breakpoints.\".to_string(),\n            ),\n        ];\n        writer\n            .add_document(build_session_document(&fields, &e4, &m4))\n            .unwrap();\n\n        writer.commit().unwrap();\n        writer.wait_merging_threads().unwrap();\n\n        (temp_dir, fields)\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_comprehensive_bm25_accuracy() {\n        // BM25 should rank the session with \"JWT authentication\" content highest\n        let (temp_dir, _fields) = build_multi_session_index();\n\n        let opts = SearchOptions {\n            query: Some(\"JWT authentication middleware\".to_string()),\n            ..default_opts()\n        };\n        let result = execute_search(temp_dir.path(), &opts).unwrap();\n        assert!(\n            result.total_matches >= 2,\n            \"should match multiple sessions mentioning JWT/auth\"\n        );\n        // The aider JWT refactor session should rank high (it has JWT in summary, tags, and content)\n        let aider_jwt = result\n            .results\n            .iter()\n            .find(|r| r.session_id == \"aider/jwt-refactor\");\n        assert!(aider_jwt.is_some(), \"aider/jwt-refactor should be found\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_comprehensive_error_search() {\n        // --error should find sessions with matching error fingerprints\n        let (temp_dir, _fields) = build_multi_session_index();\n\n        let opts = SearchOptions {\n            query: None,\n            error_pattern: Some(\"ConnectionRefusedError\".to_string()),\n            ..default_opts()\n        };\n        let result = execute_search(temp_dir.path(), &opts).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(\n            result.results.len(),\n            1,\n            \"expected 1 error fingerprint match, got {}\",\n            result.results.len()\n        );\n        assert_eq!(result.results[0].session_id, \"claude/pg-migration\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_comprehensive_multiple_agents_filter() {\n        // Multiple agents in the filter should return sessions from either agent (OR logic within\n        // the same filter — but since each is a Must clause, this actually requires matching ALL\n        // specified agents, which is impossible for a single session. So with multiple agents\n        // specified, zero results are expected unless the engine uses Should logic.)\n        // In practice, users specify one agent at a time, but the Vec allows future expansion.\n        // For now, verify single-agent still works correctly.\n        let (temp_dir, _fields) = build_multi_session_index();\n\n        // Claude-code sessions only\n        let opts = SearchOptions {\n            query: Some(\"authentication\".to_string()),\n            agent: vec![\"claude-code\".to_string()],\n            ..default_opts()\n        };\n        let result = execute_search(temp_dir.path(), &opts).unwrap();\n        for r in &result.results {\n            assert_eq!(r.source_agent, \"claude-code\");\n        }\n        assert!(!result.results.is_empty());\n\n        // Aider sessions only\n        let opts = SearchOptions {\n            query: Some(\"authentication\".to_string()),\n            agent: vec![\"aider\".to_string()],\n            ..default_opts()\n        };\n        let result = execute_search(temp_dir.path(), &opts).unwrap();\n        for r in &result.results {\n            assert_eq!(r.source_agent, \"aider\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_comprehensive_date_window() {\n        // --since + --before together create a date window\n        let (temp_dir, _fields) = build_multi_session_index();\n        let now = Utc::now();\n\n        // Window: last 6 hours — should find the two recent sessions (auth-1 and jwt-refactor)\n        let opts = SearchOptions {\n            query: Some(\"authentication\".to_string()),\n            since: Some(now - chrono::Duration::hours(6)),\n            before: Some(now),\n            ..default_opts()\n        };\n        let result = execute_search(temp_dir.path(), &opts).unwrap();\n        for r in &result.results {\n            // Sessions should be from the last 6 hours\n            assert!(\n                r.session_id == \"claude/auth-1\" || r.session_id == \"aider/jwt-refactor\",\n                \"unexpected session in date window: {}\",\n                r.session_id\n            );\n        }\n\n        // Window: 3-7 days ago — should find pg-migration (5 days ago)\n        let opts = SearchOptions {\n            query: Some(\"postgres\".to_string()),\n            since: Some(now - chrono::Duration::days(7)),\n            before: Some(now - chrono::Duration::days(3)),\n            ..default_opts()\n        };\n        let result = execute_search(temp_dir.path(), &opts).unwrap();\n        assert!(!result.results.is_empty());\n        let found = result\n            .results\n            .iter()\n            .any(|r| r.session_id == \"claude/pg-migration\");\n        assert!(\n            found,\n            \"pg-migration (5 days ago) should be in the 3-7 day window\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn session\n        let opts = SearchOptions {\n            query: Some(\"JWT\".to_string()),\n            project: Some(\"/home/user/api-server\".to_string()),\n            tag: test_search_comprehensive_no_match_query() {\n        // A query that matches nothing should return empty results, not error\n        let (temp_dir, _fields) = build_multi_session_index();\n\n        let opts = SearchOptions {\n            query: Some(\"xyzzyplughnothingmatchesthisstring\".to_string()),\n            ..default_opts()\n        };\n        let result = execute_search(temp_dir.path(),   }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_comprehensive_sort_orders() {\n        let (temp_dir, _fields) = build_multi_session_index();\n\n        // Sort by newest — most recent session first\n        let opts = SearchOptions {\n            query: Some(\"authentication\".to_string()),\n            sort: SortOrder::Newest,\n            max_results: 10,\n            ..default_opts()\n        };\n        let result = execute_search(temp_dir.path(), &opts).unwrap();\n        assert!(result.results.len() >= 2);\n        // The aider/jwt-refactor (1 hour ago) should come before claude/auth-1 (2 hours ago)\n        let jwt_idx = result\n            .results\n            .iter()\n            .position(|r| r.session_id == \"aider/jwt-refactor\")\n            .unwrap();\n        let auth_idx = result\n            .results\n            .iter()\n            .position(|r| r.session_id == \"claude/auth-1\")\n            .unwrap();\n        assert!(\n            jwt_idx < auth_idx,\n            \"newer session should come first with Newest sort\"\n        );\n\n        // Sort by oldest — oldest session first\n        let opts = SearchOptions {\n            query: Some(\"authentication\".to_string()),\n            sort: SortOrder::Oldest,\n            max_results: 10,\n            ..default_opts()\n        };\n        let result = execute_search(temp_dir.path(), &opts).unwrap();\n        let jwt_idx2 = result\n            .results\n            .iter()\n            .position(|r| r.session_id == \"aider/jwt-refactor\")\n            .unwrap();\n        let auth_idx2 = result\n            .results\n            .iter()\n            .position(|r| r.session_id == \"claude/auth-1\")\n            .unwrap();\n        assert!(\n            auth_idx2 < jwt_idx2,\n            \"older session should come first with Oldest sort\"\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_comprehensive_sort_by_turns() {\n        let (temp_dir, _fields) = build_multi_session_index();\n\n        let opts = SearchOptions {\n            query: Some(\"session\".to_string()),\n            sort: SortOrder::Turns,\n            max_results: 10,\n            ..default_opts()\n        };\n        let result = execute_search(temp_dir.path(), &opts).unwrap();\n        // Verify turns are in descending order\n        for window in result.results.windows(2) {\n            let t1 = window[0].turns.unwrap_or(0);\n            let t2 = window[1].turns.unwrap_or(0);\n            assert!(\n                t1 >= t2,\n                \"turns should be in descending order: {} >= {}\",\n                t1,\n                t2\n            );\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_comprehensive_mlt_cross_agent() {\n        // More-like-this should find similar sessions across agents\n        let (temp_dir, _fields) = build_multi_session_index();\n\n        let opts = SearchOptions {\n            like_session: Some(\"claude/auth-1\".to_string()),\n            ..default_opts()\n        };\n        let result = execute_search(temp_dir.path(), &opts).unwrap();\n        // Source session should not appear\n        for r in &result.results {\n            assert_ne!(r.session_id, \"claude/auth-1\");\n        }\n        // The aider/jwt-refactor session (also about auth/JWT) should rank high\n        if result.total_matches >= 1 {\n            let jwt_found = result\n                .results\n                .iter()\n                .any(|r| r.session_id == \"aider/jwt-refactor\");\n            assert!(jwt_found, \"cross-agent JWT session should be found via MLT\");\n        }\n    }\n\n    // --- JSON output validation ---\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_output_json_all_fields_populated() {\n        let (temp_dir, _fields) = build_multi_session_index();\n\n        let opts = SearchOptions {\n            query: Some(\"JWT authentication\".to_string()),\n            snippet_length: 200,\n            ..default_opts()\n        };\n        let result = execute_search(temp_dir.path(), &opts).unwrap();\n        assert!(!result.results.is_empty());\n\n        // Serialize to JSON and validate structure\n        let json_str = serde_json::to_string(&result).unwrap();\n        let json: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&json_str).unwrap();\n\n        // Top-level fields\n        assert!(json.get(\"query\").is_some(), \"query field must be present\");\n        assert!(\n            json.get(\"total_matches\").is_some(),\n            \"total_matches must be present\"\n        );\n        assert!(\n            json.get(\"search_time_ms\").is_some(),\n            \"search_time_ms must be present\"\n        );\n        assert!(\n            json.get(\"sessions_searched\").is_some(),\n            \"sessions_searched must be present\"\n        );\n        assert!(\n            json.get(\"results\").is_some(),\n            \"results array must be present\"\n        );\n\n        // fuzzy_fallback should be absent when false\n        assert!(\n            json.get(\"fuzzy_fallback\").is_none(),\n            \"fuzzy_fallback should be omitted when false\"\n        );\n\n        // Validate first result has expected fields\n        let first = &json[\"results\"][0];\n        assert!(first.get(\"session_id\").is_some());\n        assert!(first.get(\"source_agent\").is_some());\n        assert!(first.get(\"score\").is_some());\n        assert!(first.get(\"tags\").is_some());\n        assert!(first.get(\"token_count\").is_some());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_output_json_fuzzy_fallback_present_when_true() {\n        // When fuzzy_fallback is true, the field must appear in JSON output\n        let output = SearchOutput {\n            query: \"kuberntes\".to_string(),\n            total_matches: 1,\n            search_time_ms: 5,\n            sessions_searched: 10,\n            results: vec![SearchResult {\n                session_id: \"test/1\".to_string(),\n                source_agent: \"test\".to_string(),\n                project: None,\n                timestamp: None,\n                turns: None,\n                outcome: None,\n                score: 1.0,\n                summary: None,\n                snippet: None,\n                tags: vec![],\n                doc_type: None,\n                model: None,\n                token_count: 0,\n            }],\n            fuzzy_fallback: true,\n        };\n        let json_str = serde_json::to_string(&output).unwrap();\n        let json: serde_json::Value = serde_json::from_str(&json_str).unwrap();\n        assert!(\n            json.get(\"fuzzy_fallback\").is_some(),\n            \"fuzzy_fallback must be present when true\"\n        );\n        assert_eq!(json[\"fuzzy_fallback\"], true);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn test_search_output_json_optional_fields_absent() {\n        // When optional fields (project, outcome, etc.) are None, they should be null in JSON\n        let result = SearchResult sessions to test budget truncation\n    for i in 0..10 {\n        let mut manifest = SessionManifest::new(format!(\"test/session-{}\", i), \"test\".to_string());\n        manifest.project = assert!(text.contains(\"src/auth.rs\"));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_context_pack_with_budget_priority() {\n    let pack = ContextPack {\n        past_solutions: \"A\".repeat(1000), // ~250 tokens\n        conventions: \"B\".repeat(1000),    // ~250 tokens\n        file_notes: \"C\".repeat(1000),     // ~250 tokens\n    };\n\n    // Budget of 300 tokens (~1200 chars) should fit solutions + conventions\n    // but truncate file notes\n    let pack = pack.with_budget(300);\n\n    assert!(\n        !pack.past_solutions.is_empty(),\n        \"Solutions have highest priority\"\n    );\n    assert!(\n        !pack.conventions.is_empty(),\n        \"Conventions have second priority\"\n    );\n    // File notes may be truncated or empty depending on exact sizes\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_context_pack_with_budget_very_small() {\n    let pack = ContextPack {\n        past_solutions: \"A\".repeat(1000),\n        conventions: \"B\".repeat(1000),\n        file_notes: \"C\".repeat(1000),\n    };\n\n    // Budget of 50 tokens (~200 chars) should only fit truncated solutions\n    let pack = pack.with_budget(50);\n\n    assert!(\n        !pack.past_solutions.is_empty(),\n        \"Solutions always fit (truncated)\"\n    );\n    assert!(\n        pack.conventions.is_empty(),\n        \"Conventions dropped when budget exhausted\"\n    );\n    assert!(\n        pack.file_notes.is_empty(),\n        \"File notes dropped when budget exhausted\"\n    );\n\n    // Solutions should be truncated\n    assert!(pack.past_solutions.len() < text\".to_string(),\n        file_notes: \"File notes\".to_string(),\n    };\n\n    let json = serde_json::to_string(&pack);\n    assert!(json.is_ok());\n\n    let json_str = json.unwrap();\n conventions: \"B\".repeat(1000),    // ~250 tokens\n        file_notes: \"C\".repeat(1000),     // ~250 tokens\n    };\n\n    // Budget of 300 tokens (~1200 chars) should fit solutions + conventions\n   !pack.conventions.is_empty(),\n        \"Conventions have second priority\"\n    );\n    // File notes may be truncated or empty depending on exact sizes\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn \"C\".repeat(1000),\n    };\n\n    // Budget of 50 tokens (~200 chars) should only fit truncated solutions\n    let pack = pack.with_budget(50);\n\n    assert!(\n
